name: CORNER_MODE
description: The corner mode is a function that allows the program to control the cornering action of advanced speed control moves (those suffixed “SP”). Automatic corner speed control is any move type where the end angle of the previous move differs from the start angle of the next move (typically vectors), and allows the system to automatically reduce the speed based on DECEL_ANGLE and STOP_ANGLE . Note that corner speed control operates by setting the next move’s STARTMOVE_SPEED, and so the AXIS_BLENDING must be set to a mode that interrogates STARTMOVE_SPEED and uses the lowest value at the move boundary (because corner control requires a reduction in speed). Consequently, corner control will not function if AXIS_BLENDING is set to 0 (off), 2 (previous) or 4 (high). Automatic radius speed control is for moves that have a radius (like MOVECIRCSP, MHELICALSP and MSPHERICALSP) and allows the system to reduce the speed around a radius depending on its tightness. This can be achieved in two ways, either by factoring the speed by the ratio of the corner’s radius to the FULL_SP_RADIUS , or by limiting the centripetal acceleration (v 2 / r), and this can either cap the centripetal acceleration to that at FULL_SP_RADIUS or to ACCEL . The ratio of radii method is for backward compatibility, as it does not accurately model the forces due to the severity of the radius, which the centripetal mode does. Note that radius speed control operates by overriding each move’s FORCE_SPEED and so, unlike corner speed control, is not dependant on the setting of AXIS_BLENDING. Automatic radius speed control can also be used with vectors, like MOVESP and MOVEABSSP, where the change in direction between two vectors is used to determine an effective radius that is used as per automatic radius speed control. The purpose of this is for the case of densely packed vectors that are almost inline, but which together describe a rapid change of direction, a case that conventional corner control using STOP_ANGLE and DECEL_ANGLE will not handle. This is intended to be used alongside conventional corner control. You can enable any combination of the speed control bits, but bit 4 is only significant if bit 3 is set and bit 5 is only significant if bit 4 is set. When bits 4 and 5 are set (centripetal mode with centripetal acceleration limited to ACCEL), the value of FULL_SP_RADIUS is not used and may be left at its default value of zero. Note that corner control is processed when moves are loaded using current parameter values, and so are not subject to change. The CORNER_STATE machine allows interaction with a TrioBASIC program and the loading of buffered moves depending on RAISE_ANGLE
examples:
CORNER_MODE = 2 + 4
CORNER_MODE.1 = ON
CORNER_MODE.2 = ON
CORNER_MODE.3 = ON
CORNER_MODE.4 = OFF
CORNER_MODE.3 = ON
CORNER_MODE.4 = ON
CORNER_MODE.5 = OFF
CORNER_MODE.3 = ON
CORNER_MODE.4 = ON
CORNER_MODE.5 = ON
DEFCONST radius 4 'The radius of the semi-circle





DIM n, i AS INTEGER


DIM angle, x, y, x0, y0 AS FLOAT





CHANNEL_WRITE(9, "%[USER] SCOPE ARM")


WA(2000)


    


INITIALISE





FOR i = 0 TO 1


    BASE(i)


    ATYPE = 0


    UNITS = 1000000


    SPEED = 100


    ACCEL = 1000


    DECEL = 1000


    JERK = 10000


    SERVO = ON


    STARTMOVE_SPEED = -1


    ENDMOVE_SPEED = -1


    AXIS_MODE.2 = 0 '0 = 2D mode; 1 = 3D mode


    LIMIT_BUFFERED = BUFFERSIZE


    MERGE = ON


    DEFPOS(0)


NEXT i





BASE(0, 1)





CORNER_MODE.1 = 1 '1 = Automatic corner speed control enabled


CORNER_MODE.3 = 1 '1 = Automatic radius speed control enabled


CORNER_MODE.4 = 1 '0 = FULL_SP_RADIUS acceleration; 1 = Centripetal
acceleration


CORNER_MODE.5 = 1 '0 = Limit to FULL_SP_RADIUS; 1 = not exceed ACCEL


CORNER_MODE.6 = 1 '0 = Vector radius speed control off; 1 = on





n = BUFFERSIZE


FORCE_SPEED = SPEED





TRIGGER


WA(500)





'Vector up, semi-circle, vector down, semi-circle (composed of many small
vectors), vector up


MOVESP(0, 100)


MOVECIRCSP(2 * radius, 0, radius, 0, 1)


MOVESP(0, -100)


x0 = -radius


y0 = 0


FOR i = 1 TO n


    angle = -PI * (n - i) / n


    x = radius * COS(angle)


    y = radius * SIN(angle)


    MOVESP(x - x0, y - y0)


    x0 = x


    y0 = y


NEXT i


MOVESP(0, 100)





WAIT IDLE





WA(500)


CHANNEL_WRITE(9, "%[USER] SCOPE DISARM")
CORNER_MODE = 2 + 4

CORNER_MODE.1 = ON
CORNER_MODE.2 = ON

CORNER_MODE.3 = ON
CORNER_MODE.4 = OFF

CORNER_MODE.3 = ON
CORNER_MODE.4 = ON
CORNER_MODE.5 = OFF

CORNER_MODE.3 = ON
CORNER_MODE.4 = ON
CORNER_MODE.5 = ON

vectors), vector up


MOVESP(0, 100)


MOVECIRCSP(2 * radius, 0, radius, 0, 1)


MOVESP(0, -100)


x0 = -radius


y0 = 0


FOR i = 1 TO n


    angle = -PI * (n - i) / n


    x = radius * COS(angle)


    y = radius * SIN(angle)


    MOVESP(x - x0, y - y0)


    x0 = x


    y0 = y


NEXT i


MOVESP(0, 100)





WAIT IDLE





WA(500)


CHANNEL_WRITE(9, "%[USER] SCOPE DISARM")

name: CORNER_STATE
description: Allows a BASIC program to interact with the move loading process. This can be used to facilitate tool adjustment such as knife rotation at sharp corners. This parameter is only active when CORNER_MODE bit 2 is set. It is also required to set bit 1 of CORNER_MODE with STOP_ANGLE set to less than or equal to RAISE_ANGLE to stop the motion.
examples:
BASE(2)
'Set up an automatic tangential move on axis 2
IF MTYPE = 0 THEN


    MOVETANG(0, 0)


ENDIF





BASE(0)


CORNER_MODE = 4 'Enable the CORNER_STATE machine


RAISE_ANGLE = 15 * PI / 180 '15 degrees





REPEAT


    WAIT UNTIL CORNER_STATE > 0 'Normally CORNER_STATE = 1 here


    WAIT UNTIL MTYPE = 0 'Wait for the present XY move to finish





    'Insert BASIC commands to raise the knife here:
WA(100)





    'Setting CORNER_STATE = 3 by BASIC makes the firmware


    'load the next move but at zero speed:


    CORNER_STATE = 3





    'Firmware sets CORNER_STATE = 7 to indicate next move loaded


    WAIT UNTIL CORNER_STATE = 7
'In this example we are using MOVETANG, but if not,
then insert BASIC commands to rotate knife here:


    WA(2)
'Allows MOVETANG to start following new TANG_DIRECTION


    WAIT UNTIL REMAIN AXIS(2) = 0





    'Insert BASIC commands to lower the knife here:
WA(100)





    'Set CORNER_MODE = 0 to ramp up speed in XY


    CORNER_STATE = 0


UNTIL FALSE
LIMIT_BUFFERED = 10


  BASE(0, 1)


  MERGE = ON
MOVEABS(200, 0)


  MOVEABS(200, 200)


  MOVEABS(0, 200)


  MOVEABS(0, 0)

name: CO_READ
syntax: CO_READ(slot, address, index, subindex, type[, vr_number])
description: This function gets a CANopen-over-EtherCAT (CoE) object from the remote drive or I/O device. The object’s index and sub-index are used to request a value and that value is either placed in the VR or is displayed in the Motion Perfect terminal if the VR number is set to -1. Refer to the remote device’s manual for a list of available objects. If the object value is returned successfully, the command returns TRUE (-1). Otherwise, in the case of an error while requesting the value, the command returns FALSE (0).
params: Slot number of the EtherCAT module.
Node address of the remote device on the network

VR number between 0 and max VR where the result will be
  stored.


  (-1 means the value will be printed to the terminal)

name: CO_READ_AXIS
description: This function gets a CANopen-over-EtherCAT (CoE) object from the remote drive or I/O device. The object’s index and sub-index are used to request a value and that value is either placed in the VR or is displayed in the Motion Perfect terminal if the VR number is set to -1. Refer to the remote device’s manual for a list of available objects. If the object value is returned successfully, the command returns TRUE (-1) . Otherwise, in the case of an error while requesting the value, the command returns FALSE (0).
params: Axis number of the EtherCAT drive.

VR number between 0 and max VR where the result will be
  stored.


  (-1 means the value will be printed to the terminal)

name: CO_WRITE
syntax: CO_WRITE(slot, address, index, subindex, type, vr_number[, value])
description: This function sets a CANopen-over-EtherCAT (CoE) object in the remote drive or I/O device. The object’s index and sub-index are used to write a value to that object. The value can come from a VR or is put into the command directly if the VR number is set to -1. Refer to the remote device’s manual for a list of available objects. If the object value is set successfully, the command returns TRUE (-1). Otherwise, in the case of an error while writing the value, the command returns FALSE (0).
params: Slot number of the EtherCAT module.
Node address of the remote device on the network

VR number between 0 and max VR where the result will be
  stored.


  (-1 if the next parameter contains the value to be written)
Optional data value for direct setting of the object

name: CO_WRITE_AXIS
syntax: CO_WRITE_AXIS(axis_number, index, subindex, type, vr_number[, value])
description: This function sets a CANopen-over-EtherCAT (CoE) object in the remote drive or I/O device. The object’s index and sub-index are used to write a value to that object. The value can come from a VR or is put into the command directly if the VR number is set to -1. Refer to the remote device’s manual for a list of available objects. If the object value is set successfully, the command returns TRUE (-1). Otherwise, in the case of an error while writing the value, the command returns FALSE (0).
params: Axis number of the EtherCAT drive.

VR number between 0 and max VR where the result will be
  stored.


  (-1 if the next parameter contains the value to be written)
Optional data value for direct setting of the object

name: CRC16
description: Calculates a 16 bit Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC) of data stored in contiguous Table Memory or VR Memory locations.
examples:

'Load flush data into TABLE memory at location 8-9

'Load flush data into VR memory at location 0

reginit = 0

poly=$8005

name: CREEP
description: Sets the CREEP speed on the current base axis. The creep speed is used for the slow part of a DATUM sequence.
examples:
BASE(2)
CREEP = 10
SPEED = 500
DATUM(4)
CREEP AXIS(1) = 10
SPEED AXIS(1) = 500
DATUM(4) AXIS(1)

name: C_SPLINE
syntax:
C_SPLINE(mode, index-in, number-points, dimensions expand-ratio,
index-out{, tension, bias})
description: The C_SPLINE command operates in two modes using Cubic Hermite splines to extend the source points into a smoother higher resolution profile. This can be used to smooth motion profiles which can be run in a sequence using MOVEABS, MOVABSSEQ or similar. The expand parameter gives the number of points that each position is expanded by. So the total number of output points is (((number of points -1) * expand) + 1)
params:
The location of the input points in 
TABLE

  memory (grouped for example x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, y3, …)

The number of input points in 
TABLE

  memory (Each point has 2..8 dimensions)
The number of dimensions of the data (2..8)

The number of intermediate points generated between each
  input point (2 to 100)

The location of the output points in 
TABLE

  memory (Format depends on “mode”)

Used for Cardinal splines only. (-1 to 1) -1 is “loosest”,
  1 is “tightest”
Used for Cardinal splines only. (-1 to 1, normally 0)

name: : Colon
description: The colon character is used as a label terminator and as a command separator.

name: DAC
description: Writing to this parameter when SERVO = OFF and AXIS_ENABLE = ON allows the user to force a demand value for that axis. On an analogue axis this will set a voltage on the output. On a digital axis this will be the demand value. For example, an EtherCAT CSV axis that has target speed. When using a FlexAxis as a stepper or encoder output or anytime with SERVO = OFF the voltage outputs are available for user control. The WDOG and AXIS_ENABLE must be ON for the demand value to be set. When the WDOG or AXIS_ENABLE is OFF you can write a value to DAC but the actual output ( DAC_OUT ) will be at 0. The maximum output value is limited by OUTLIMIT. Set OUTLIMIT to a suitable value that matches the axis type and the connected device.
examples:
WDOG = ON
SERVO AXIS(0) = OFF
square:
DAC AXIS(0) = 1024
WA(250)
DAC AXIS(0) = -1024
WA(250)
GOTO square

name: DAC_OUT
description: This parameter returns the demand value for the axis. In an analogue system this will be the value sent to the voltage output (the D to A converter). If SERVO = ON this is the output of the closed loop algorithm. If SERVO = OFF it is the value set by the user in DAC . DAC_OUT value, and hence the actual output is limited by the setting in OUTLIMIT . In a digital system DAC_OUT returns the demand value for the axis which could be the Target Position, Target Speed or Target Torque depending on the axis ATYPE .
examples:
>>PRINT DAC_OUT AXIS(8)
288.0000
>>
ax = 2


PRINT "Target position in PDO = "; DAC_OUT AXIS(ax)


CO_READ_AXIS(ax, $607A, 0, 4, 20)
PRINT "Target position in drive = "; VR(20)
ax = 2


PRINT "Target velocity in PDO = "; DAC_OUT AXIS(ax)


CO_READ_AXIS(ax, $60FF, 0, 4, 20)
PRINT "Target velocity in drive = "; VR(20)

>>PRINT DAC_OUT AXIS(8)
288.0000
>>

ax = 2


PRINT "Target position in PDO = "; DAC_OUT AXIS(ax)


CO_READ_AXIS(ax, $607A, 0, 4, 20)
PRINT "Target position in drive = "; VR(20)

ax = 2


PRINT "Target velocity in PDO = "; DAC_OUT AXIS(ax)


CO_READ_AXIS(ax, $60FF, 0, 4, 20)
PRINT "Target velocity in drive = "; VR(20)

name: DAC_SCALE
description: DAC_SCALE is an integer that is multiplied to the output of the closed loop algorithm. You can use it to reverse the polarity of the demand value or to scale it so to effectively reduce the resolution of the closed loop algorithm. As it is applied to the output of the closed loop algorithm it is not applied to position based axis.
examples:
BASE(2) ' set axis 2 to work in reverse direction
DAC_SCALE = -1
ENCODER_RATIO(-1,1)

BASE(2) ' set axis 2 to work in reverse direction
DAC_SCALE = -1
ENCODER_RATIO(-1,1)

name: DATE$
description: DATE$ is used as part of a PRINT statement or a STRING variable to write the current date from the real time clock. The date is printed in the format DD/MMM/YYYY. The month is displayed in short text form. The DATE$ is set through the DATE command
examples:
PRINT #5, DATE$
DIM string1 AS STRING(30)
string1 = "Error occurred on the " + DATE$
PRINT #5, DATE$

DIM string1 AS STRING(30)
string1 = "Error occurred on the " + DATE$

name: DATE
description: Returns or sets the current date held by the real time clock.

name: DATUM
description: Performs one of 6 homing sequences to locate an axis to an absolute position. The creep speed used in the sequences is set using CREEP and the programmed speed is set with the SPEED command. When the datum sequence has been completed, the axis will be at the home position in the case of functions 3 and 4, or will stop a small distance after the home position when using functions 1, 2, 5 or 6. DATUM(0) and DATUM(7) are special cases that will reset the motion system after an axis critical error. It leaves the positions in DPOS and MPOS unchanged.
examples:
FORWARD              'start production line
WHILE IN(2)=ON
IF MOTION_ERROR=0 THEN
OP(8,ON)        'green light on; line is in motion
ELSE
OP(8, OFF)
GOSUB error_correct
ENDIF
WEND
CANCEL
STOP
error_correct:
REPEAT
OP(10,ON)
WA(250)
OP(10,OFF)         'flash red light to show crash
WA(250)
UNTIL IN(1)=OFF
DATUM(0)             'reset axis status errors
SERVO=ON             'turn the servo back on
WDOG=ON              'turn on the watchdog
OP(9,ON)             'sound siren that line will restart
WA(1000)
OP(9,OFF)
FORWARD              'restart motion
RETURN
SERVO=ON
WDOG=ON
CREEP=1000    'set the search speed
SPEED=5000    'set the return speed
DATUM(1)      'register on Z mark and sets this to datum
WAIT IDLE
MOVEABS (0)    'moves to datum position
SERVO=ON
WDOG=ON
REV_IN=-1   'temporarily turn off the limit switch
function
DATUM_IN=5  'sets input 5 for registration
SPEED=5000  'set speed, for quick location of limit switch
CREEP=500   'set creep speed for slow move to find edge of
switch
DATUM(4)    'find “edge” at creep speed and stop
WAIT IDLE
DATUM_IN=-1
REV_IN=5    'restore input 5 as a limit switch again
SERVO=ON
WDOG=ON
DATUM_IN=7  'sets input 7 as home switch
SPEED=5000  'set speed, for quick location of switch
CREEP=500   'set creep speed for slow move to find edge of
switch
DATUM(5)    'start the homing sequence
WAIT IDLE

FORWARD              'start production line
WHILE IN(2)=ON
IF MOTION_ERROR=0 THEN
OP(8,ON)        'green light on; line is in motion
ELSE
OP(8, OFF)
GOSUB error_correct
ENDIF
WEND
CANCEL
STOP
error_correct:
REPEAT
OP(10,ON)
WA(250)
OP(10,OFF)         'flash red light to show crash
WA(250)
UNTIL IN(1)=OFF
DATUM(0)             'reset axis status errors
SERVO=ON             'turn the servo back on
WDOG=ON              'turn on the watchdog
OP(9,ON)             'sound siren that line will restart
WA(1000)
OP(9,OFF)
FORWARD              'restart motion
RETURN

SERVO=ON
WDOG=ON
CREEP=1000    'set the search speed
SPEED=5000    'set the return speed
DATUM(1)      'register on Z mark and sets this to datum
WAIT IDLE
MOVEABS (0)    'moves to datum position

SERVO=ON
WDOG=ON
REV_IN=-1   'temporarily turn off the limit switch
function
DATUM_IN=5  'sets input 5 for registration
SPEED=5000  'set speed, for quick location of limit switch
CREEP=500   'set creep speed for slow move to find edge of
switch
DATUM(4)    'find “edge” at creep speed and stop
WAIT IDLE
DATUM_IN=-1
REV_IN=5    'restore input 5 as a limit switch again

SERVO=ON
WDOG=ON
DATUM_IN=7  'sets input 7 as home switch
SPEED=5000  'set speed, for quick location of switch
CREEP=500   'set creep speed for slow move to find edge of
switch
DATUM(5)    'start the homing sequence
WAIT IDLE

BASE(x_axis)
INVERT_IN(205, 1)

BASE(y_axis)


INVERT_IN(206, 1)


DATUM_IN = 206

name: DEBUG_LOG
description: Records up to 4096 of the most recent controller optional events. Each event is recorded with a timestamp of the current RTC value and the time since power-up of the controller. Each event is also tagged with a checksum for verification. When executing the command, either in the terminal or a Trio BASIC program, it will print the recorded event messages to the terminal. Note : Before displaying a log entry the checksum for the log is validated, if it is invalid then the message '<INVALID LOG ENTRY>' will be displayed instead.
params:
Displays all debug log entries starting from line
  number 'start'.

Special case, erases log from flash memory (second
  parameter is not needed).

Displays all debug log entries starting from line
  number 'start' until line number 'finish'.
examples: DEBUG_LOG(1,7)

name: DEBUG_LOG_MODE
description: This parameter enables debug logging. Information is saved in the debug log in the flash memory and can be retrieved by typing DEBUG_LOG.
examples: DEBUG_LOG_MODE.1 = 1
DEBUG_LOG_MODE.1 = 1

name: DECEL
description: The DECEL axis parameter may be used to set or read back the deceleration rate of each axis fitted.
examples:
DECEL=100' Set deceleration rate
PRINT " Decel is ";DECEL;" mm/sec/sec"

name: DECEL_ANGLE
description: This parameter is used with CORNER_MODE , it defines the maximum change in direction of a 2-axis interpolated move that will be merged at full speed. When the change in direction (see CHANGE_DIR_LAST) between two moves is greater than DECEL_ANGLE, the speed will be proportionally reduced using the following relationship: speed = FORCE_SPEED * (angle - DECEL_ANGLE) / ( STOP_ANGLE - DECEL_ANGLE) When a move needs to slow down, its STARTMOVE_SPEED is set to the above value.
examples:
CORNER_MODE.1 = 1
DECEL_ANGLE = 15 * PI / 180
STOP_ANGLE = 45 * PI / 180

name: DEFCONST
description: DEFCONST defines a literal name with a value, either integer or floating point. These literals can then be used with array declarations or switch statements or any general line of BASIC. They can be added to INCLUDE files and referenced within the INCLUDE file itself or the main program that uses the INCLUDE file. DEFCONST is evaluated at compile time. This limits its use for certain runtime functionality. For example, “DEFCONST process_number PROCNUMBER” will not work since DEFCONST does not know which process the program is on in runtime.
params:
Any user-defined name containing lower case alpha,
  numerical or underscore (_) characters.
The value or evaluated expression assigned to the name.
examples:
DEFCONST mysize 20
DEFCONST fval SIN(90)
DEFCONST i1 7
DEFCONST i2 i1 * 1000
DIM myarray AS INTEGER(mysize)
DEFCONST errorval 100
SELECT_CASE swval
CASE 0
PRINT "valid data"
CASE errorval
PRINT "SWVAL invalid"
END_CASE

DEFCONST mysize 20
DEFCONST fval SIN(90)
DEFCONST i1 7
DEFCONST i2 i1 * 1000
DIM myarray AS INTEGER(mysize)

DEFCONST errorval 100
SELECT_CASE swval
CASE 0
PRINT "valid data"
CASE errorval
PRINT "SWVAL invalid"
END_CASE

name: DEFPOS
description: Defines the current position(s) as a new absolute value. The positions specified are placed in DPOS , while MPOS is adjusted to maintain the FE value. This function is completed after the next servo-cycle. DEFPOS may be used at any time, even while a move is in progress, but its normal function is to set the position values of a group of axes that are stationary.
params:
Absolute position to set on current base axis in user
  units.

Absolute position to set on the next axis in 
BASE
 array in user units.

Absolute position to set on the next axis in 
BASE
 array in user units.
examples:
DATUM(5) AXIS(1) 'Home both axes. At the end of the DATUM
DATUM(4) AXIS(3) 'procedure, the positions will be 0, 0
WAIT IDLE AXIS(1)
WAIT IDLE AXIS(3)
BASE(1, 3) 'Set up the BASE array
DEFPOS(-10, -35) 'Define positions of axes to -10 and -35
DEFPOS(10)
WAIT UNTIL OFFPOS = 0 'Ensures DEFPOS has completed
MOVEABS(25.03)
>>BASE(0)
>>DEFPOS(0, 0, 0, 0)
>>

DATUM(5) AXIS(1) 'Home both axes. At the end of the DATUM
DATUM(4) AXIS(3) 'procedure, the positions will be 0, 0
WAIT IDLE AXIS(1)
WAIT IDLE AXIS(3)
BASE(1, 3) 'Set up the BASE array
DEFPOS(-10, -35) 'Define positions of axes to -10 and -35

DEFPOS(10)
WAIT UNTIL OFFPOS = 0 'Ensures DEFPOS has completed
MOVEABS(25.03)

name: DEVICENET
description: The command DEVICENET is used to start and stop the DeviceNet slave function which is built into the Motion Coordinator. Polled I/O data is transferred periodically: From PLC to [ TABLE (poll_base) -> TABLE (poll_base + poll_in)] To PLC from [ TABLE (poll_base + poll_in + 1) -> TABLE (poll_base + poll_in + poll_out)]
params: Start the DeviceNet slave protocol on the given slot.
Put start-up baud rate into Flash EPROM
examples:
DEVICENET(-1, 0, 500, 30, 0, 4, 2)
DEVICENET(2, 1)
DEVICENET(0, 2, 125)
DEVICENET(2, 1)
DEVICENET(0, 2, 125)

name: DIM
syntax:

DIM name AS type(size1[, size2[, size3]])
description: Declare a variable as a BOOLEAN value. This can be used with TRUE and FALSE , any non-zero value written to a BOOLEAN variable will set its state to TRUE .
params:
Any user-defined name containing lower case alpha, numerical
  or underscore (_) characters.

The number of elements in each dimension of the array,
  up to 3 dimensions.
examples:
DIM machine_ok AS BOOLEAN
machine_ok = TRUE
WHILE machine_ok
IF MOTION_ERROR <> 0 AND IN(0) THEN
machine_ok = FALSE
ENDIF
WEND

name: DIR
description: Prints a list of all programs including their size and RUNTYPE .
params: Directory listing of SD card memory
Directory listing of USB memory stick. (Flex-7 only)

Extended listing of controller memory (used by 
Motion
  Perfect).

name: DISABLE_GROUP
description: Used to create a group of axes which will be disabled if there is a motion error in one or more of the group. After the group is created, when an error occurs all the axes in the group will have their AXIS_ENABLE set to OFF and SERVO set to OFF . Multiple groups can be made, although one axis cannot belong to more than one group. Only axes that have individual enables should be used in a disable group. Such as Digital drives and Steppers.
examples:
DISABLE_GROUP(-1)          'remove any previous axis
groupings
DISABLE_GROUP(0, 1, 2, 6)  'group axes 0 to 2 and 6
DISABLE_GROUP(3, 4, 5, 7)  'group axes 3 to 5 and 7
WDOG = ON 'turn on the enable relay and the remote drive
enable
FOR ax = 0 TO 7
AXIS_ENABLE AXIS(ax) = ON 'enable the 8 axes
SERVO AXIS(ax) = ON       'start position loop servo for
each axis
NEXT ax
DISABLE_GROUP(0) 'put axis 0 in its own group
DISABLE_GROUP(1) 'put axis 1 in another group
GOSUB group_enable0
GOSUB group_enable1
WDOG = ON
FORWARD AXIS(0)
FORWARD AXIS(1)
WHILE TRUE
IF AXIS_ENABLE AXIS(0) = 0 THEN
PRINT "motion error axis 0"
reset_0_flag = 1
ENDIF
IF AXIS_ENABLE AXIS(1) = 0 THEN
PRINT "motion error axis 1"
reset_1_flag = 1
ENDIF
IF reset_0_flag = 1 AND IN(0) = ON THEN
GOSUB group_enable0
FORWARD AXIS(0)
reset_0_flag = 0
ENDIF
IF reset_1_flag = 1 AND IN(1) = ON THEN
GOSUB group_enable1
FORWARD AXIS(1)
reset_1_flag = 0
ENDIF
WEND
group_enable0:
BASE(0)
DATUM(7) 'clear motion error on axis 0
WA(10)
AXIS_ENABLE = ON
SERVO = ON
RETURN
group_enable1:
BASE(1)
DATUM(7) 'clear motion error on axis 0
WA(10)
AXIS_ENABLE = ON
SERVO = ON
RETURN
DISABLE_GROUP(-1)      'remove any previous axis
groupings
DISABLE_GROUP(0, 1, 2) 'group axes 0 to 2
GOSUB group_enable     'enable the axes and clear errors
WDOG = ON
SPEED = 1000
FORWARD
WHILE IN(2) = ON   'check axis 0, but all axes in the
group
'will disable together
IF AXIS_ENABLE = 0 THEN
PRINT "Motion error in group 0"
PRINT "Press input 0 to reset"
IF IN(0) = 0 THEN     'checks if reset button is
pressed
GOSUB group_enable  'clear errors and enable axis
FORWARD             'restarts the motion
ENDIF
ENDIF
WEND
STOP            'stop program running into sub routine
group_enable:     'Clear group errors and enable axes
DATUM(0)        'clear any motion errors
WA(10)
FOR axis_no = 0 TO 2
AXIS_ENABLE AXIS(axis_no) = ON 'enable axes
SERVO AXIS(axis_no) = ON       'start position loop
servo
NEXT axis_no
RETURN

DISABLE_GROUP(-1)          'remove any previous axis
groupings
DISABLE_GROUP(0, 1, 2, 6)  'group axes 0 to 2 and 6
DISABLE_GROUP(3, 4, 5, 7)  'group axes 3 to 5 and 7
WDOG = ON 'turn on the enable relay and the remote drive
enable
FOR ax = 0 TO 7
AXIS_ENABLE AXIS(ax) = ON 'enable the 8 axes
SERVO AXIS(ax) = ON       'start position loop servo for
each axis
NEXT ax

DISABLE_GROUP(0) 'put axis 0 in its own group
DISABLE_GROUP(1) 'put axis 1 in another group
GOSUB group_enable0
GOSUB group_enable1
WDOG = ON
FORWARD AXIS(0)
FORWARD AXIS(1)
WHILE TRUE
IF AXIS_ENABLE AXIS(0) = 0 THEN
PRINT "motion error axis 0"
reset_0_flag = 1
ENDIF
IF AXIS_ENABLE AXIS(1) = 0 THEN
PRINT "motion error axis 1"
reset_1_flag = 1
ENDIF
IF reset_0_flag = 1 AND IN(0) = ON THEN
GOSUB group_enable0
FORWARD AXIS(0)
reset_0_flag = 0
ENDIF
IF reset_1_flag = 1 AND IN(1) = ON THEN
GOSUB group_enable1
FORWARD AXIS(1)
reset_1_flag = 0
ENDIF
WEND
group_enable0:
BASE(0)
DATUM(7) 'clear motion error on axis 0
WA(10)
AXIS_ENABLE = ON
SERVO = ON
RETURN
group_enable1:
BASE(1)
DATUM(7) 'clear motion error on axis 0
WA(10)
AXIS_ENABLE = ON
SERVO = ON
RETURN

DISABLE_GROUP(-1)      'remove any previous axis
groupings
DISABLE_GROUP(0, 1, 2) 'group axes 0 to 2
GOSUB group_enable     'enable the axes and clear errors
WDOG = ON
SPEED = 1000
FORWARD
WHILE IN(2) = ON   'check axis 0, but all axes in the
group
'will disable together
IF AXIS_ENABLE = 0 THEN
PRINT "Motion error in group 0"
PRINT "Press input 0 to reset"
IF IN(0) = 0 THEN     'checks if reset button is
pressed
GOSUB group_enable  'clear errors and enable axis
FORWARD             'restarts the motion
ENDIF
ENDIF
WEND
STOP            'stop program running into sub routine
group_enable:     'Clear group errors and enable axes
DATUM(0)        'clear any motion errors
WA(10)
FOR axis_no = 0 TO 2
AXIS_ENABLE AXIS(axis_no) = ON 'enable axes
SERVO AXIS(axis_no) = ON       'start position loop
servo
NEXT axis_no
RETURN

name: DISPLAY
description: Determines which group of the I/O channels are to be displayed on the LCD or LED bank.

name: DLINK
description: This is a specialised command, to allow access to the SLM™ digital drive interface. The axis parameters have to be initialised by the DLINK function 2 command before the interface can be used for controlling an external drive. The current SLM software dictates that the drive MUST be powered up after power is applied to the Motion Coordinator/ SLM.
params: Check for presence of SLM servo drive

Assign a 
Motion Coordinator axis to a SLM
  channel

Returns slot and communication channel associated with
  an axis

name: DPOS
description: The demand position DPOS is the demanded axis position generated by the motion commands. DPOS is set to MPOS when SERVO or WDOG are OFF DPOS can be adjusted without any motion by using DEFPOS or OFFPOS . A step change in DPOS can be written using ENDMOVE

name: DRIVEIO_BASE
description: This parameter sets the start address of any drive I/O channels. Together with CANIO_BASE , MODULEIO_BASE and NODE_IO the I/O allocation scheme can replace and expand the behaviour of MODULE_IO_MODE .

name: DRIVE_BRAKE_OUTPUT
description: Assign the Motion Coordinator output to operate as the brake control. The digital output allocated to this function will not be available for use in programs via OP command, PSWITCH or as a PLC output. Brake control sequences and timing are set in the drive using configuration software. For example in the Motion Perfect DX5 setup pages. DRIVE_BRAKE_OUTPUT supports specific EtherCAT “slim” drives where brake control is delegated to the EtherCAT master.
examples:
DRIVE_BRAKE_OUTPUT AXIS(0) = 32


DRIVE_BRAKE_OUTPUT AXIS(1) = 33


DRIVE_BRAKE_OUTPUT AXIS(2) = 34

name: DRIVE_CLEAR
description: DRIVE_CLEAR allows the user to clear alarms in the drive. Currently this is supports only the Panasonic RTEX drives. DRIVE_READ can be used to read the value of the alarm

name: DRIVE_CONTROL
syntax:
DRIVE_CONTROL AXIS(n) = parameter_value


 


return_value = DRIVE_CONTROL AXIS(n)
description: This command is for support of the Omron G5 drive over EtherCAT. The setting value identifies the object to be watched by DRIVE_MONITOR at runtime every servo period. For this drive, a fixed mapping is used to implement DRIVE_MONITOR and DRIVE_CONTROL. Code CANopen object Description 01 603Fh-00h Error code 02 6041h-00h Status word 03 6064h-00h Position actual value 04 6077h-00h Torque actual value 05 60F4h-00h Following error actual value 06 60B9h-00h Touch probe status 07 60BAh-00h Touch probe pos1 pos value 08 60BCh-00h Touch probe pos2 pos value 09 60FEh-00h Digital inputs
examples:
DRIVE_CONTROL = 5
fe_actual = DRIVE_MONITOR

name: DRIVE_CONTROLWORD
description: Sets the Control Word which is sent cyclically to a remote drive connected by a fieldbus. For example in CANopen over EtherCAT (CoE) the DRIVE_CONTROLWORD would set the value in object $6040 sub-index $00.
examples:
BASE(6)
DRIVE_CW_MODE=1 ' take manual control of the Control Word
DRIVE_CONTROLWORD = $2F ' set the bits to enable the drive

name: DRIVE_CURRENT
description: Returns the actual current value from a remote drive. For this value to be active, the cyclic data transfer from the drive must be first configured to return the drive actual current value. For a drive connected by CANopen over EtherCAT (CoE) the value will be configured as part of the Process Data Object (PDO).
examples:
PRINT #5,"Drive current value = "; DRIVE_CURRENT
AXIS(2)
BASE(16)
WAIT UNTIL DRIVE_CURRENT < 500

name: DRIVE_CW_MODE
description: The operation of the control word sent cyclically to a remote drive is, by default, controlled by the firmware. For example, the control word will usually be under the control of the WDOG and AXIS_ENABLE parameters so that the drive can be enabled and disabled by software. Optionally, if DRIVE_CW_MODE is set to non-zero, the control word may be set by a user program.
examples:
BASE(0)
DRIVE_CW_MODE = 1 

'
Take manual control of the Control Word
DRIVE_CONTROLWORD = $06 'Disable the drive
WA(10)
DRIVE_CONTROLWORD = $86 'Reset the drive
WA(10)
DRIVE_CONTROLWORD = $06
BASE(2)
SERVO = OFF
DRIVE_CW_MODE = 1 'Set the control word to be user mode
DRIVE_CONTROLWORD = $06 'Disable the drive
'Set the drive to DS402 homing mode
CO_WRITE_AXIS(ax, $6060, $00, 2, -1, 6)
'Wait for the homing mode to be accepted
VR(100) = 0
REPEAT
CO_READ_AXIS(ax, $6061, $00, 2, 100)
UNTIL VR(100) = 6
'Set the homing method (1 for +ve direction, 2 for -ve)
fwd = 1
rev = 2
CO_WRITE_AXIS(ax, $6098, $00, 2, -1, fwd)
DRIVE_CONTROLWORD = $1f 'Start homing
WA(20)
'Wait for Homing Done flag (bit 12)
REPEAT
WA(1)
UNTIL DRIVE_STATUS.12 = 1
WA(20)
DEFPOS(ENCODER) 'Set the axis position to drive’s value
SERVO = ON
WDOG = ON
'Set the drive to position mode
CO_WRITE_AXIS(ax, $6060, $00, 2, -1, 8)
'Set control word to normal enabled state
DRIVE_CONTROLWORD = $2f
DRIVE_CW_MODE = 0 'Set the control word back to wdog
mode

name: DRIVE_ENABLE
description: See AXIS_ENABLE. DRIVE_ENABLE is auto-replaced by AXIS_ENABLE when loaded into later firmware versions or converted from previous Motion Coordinator product ranges.

name: DRIVE_FE
description: Returns the value of following error calculated by a remote drive in position mode. For this value to be active, the cyclic data transfer from the drive must be first configured to return the drive’s Position Error Actual value. For a drive connected by CANopen over EtherCAT (CoE) the value will be configured as part of the Process Data Object (PDO). DRIVE_FE is a more precise value of the following error than FE. With a remote drive in position mode, the bus cycle time results in the Motion Coordinator FE value increasing with speed regardless of the true following error. When DRIVE_FE is active, the DRIVE_FE_LIMIT must be set in the axis start-up as well as the normal FE_LIMIT. When ABS(DRIVE_FE) > DRIVE_FE_LIMIT, the E flag, bit 8 of AXISSTATUS is set and a MOTION_ERROR will occur.
examples:
PRINT #5,"Drive Position Error = "; DRIVE_FE
AXIS(3)
DRIVE_PROFILE AXIS(2) = 1 'Select built-in profile with
following error in the PDO
DRIVE_MODE = 1 'Select position mode (CSP)
BASE(2)
UNITS = 2 ^ 21 / 5 '21 bit encoder. Ball screw with 5 mm
pitch.


FE_LIMIT = 2.5 'Following error limit 2.5 mm
DRIVE_FE_LIMIT = 1.0 'Drive fe limit 1.0 mm
'Also set the internal position error limit in the drive
CO_WRITE_AXIS(2, $6065, 0, 7, -1, 2 ^ 20) 'Set to 0.5 x
turn

name: DRIVE_FE_LIMIT
description: This is the maximum allowable following error applied to the DRIVE_FE value, i.e., the actual following error in a remote drive which is received via a fieldbus such as EtherCAT. When exceeded the controller will generate an AXISSTATUS error, by default this will also generate a MOTION_ERROR . The MOTION_ERROR will disable the WDOG relay thus stopping further motor operation. This limit may be used to guard against fault conditions such as mechanical lock-up, loss of encoder feedback, etc. When either DRIVE_FE_LIMIT or FE_LIMIT is exceeded, bit 8 of AXISSTATUS is set. DRIVE_FE_LIMIT is required when an EtherCAT remote drive is running in position (CSP) mode and the PDO cyclic data includes the Following Error Actual value from the drive. For example, when DRIVE_PROFILE is set to 1 or 2 in the MC_CONFIG the profile includes the DRIVE_FE value. Axis Parameters in Motion Perfect may be configured to show the values of DRIVE_FE and DRIVE_FE_LIMIT.
examples:
FOR x = 0 to 4
BASE(x)
UNITS = 100
FE_LIMIT = 50
DRIVE_FE_LIMIT = 10
SPEED = 100
ACCEL = 1000
DECEL = ACCEL
NEXT x

name: DRIVE_INDEX
description: DRIVE_INDEX is used to map additional PDO parameters in the EtherCAT servo drive into VR variables. The value given is the base VR address for the mapping. The non-standard PDO parameters are mapped one per VR, starting with the first PDO parameter following the standard objects. This axis parameter can be added to the MC_CONFIG. The EtherCAT drive must be configured with an application specific profile before this function can be used.
params: The VR index where incoming PDO data will be mapped
examples:
DRIVE_INDEX = 100
'Get incoming cyclic data


user_status_1 = VR(100)


user_status_2 = VR(101)
'Set outgoing data 


VR(102) = user_control_word


VR(103) = winder_mode


VR(104) = ref_value_1


VR(105) = ref_value_2


VR(106) = correction_value


VR(107) = program_state

DRIVE_INDEX = 100
'Get incoming cyclic data


user_status_1 = VR(100)


user_status_2 = VR(101)
'Set outgoing data 


VR(102) = user_control_word


VR(103) = winder_mode


VR(104) = ref_value_1


VR(105) = ref_value_2


VR(106) = correction_value


VR(107) = program_state

name: DRIVE_INPUTS
description: Returns a numerical value that represents the bit pattern of the digital inputs on a remote axis. EtherCAT axes commonly have either a 16-bit word or 32-bit long-word in the cyclic data that is mapped to the Drive’s digital inputs. The data may also be mapped to the Motion Coordinator I/O Map.
examples:
>>BASE(2)


>>?HEX(drive_inputs)


90000
BASE(2)
WAIT UNTIL (DRIVE_INPUTS AND $80000) <> 0

>>BASE(2)


>>?HEX(drive_inputs)


90000

BASE(2)
WAIT UNTIL (DRIVE_INPUTS AND $80000) <> 0

name: DRIVE_INTERFACE
description: DRIVE_INTERFACE allows specific operations on digital drive networks. Currently this is supports only Panasonic RTEX drives.
params: The slot number to apply the function to

name: DRIVE_MODE
description: DRIVE_MODE sets the mode of operation to be used by a remote drive over EtherCAT. This MUST be set in MC_CONFIG if the EtherCAT is to be initialised on power up in the required mode. DRIVE_MODE automatically sets the drive’s mode of operation and the axis ATYPE. This axis parameter can be added to the MC_CONFIG.
params:
1: Cyclic Synchronous Position mode (CSP)


  

2: Cyclic Synchronous Velocity mode (CSV)


  

3: Cyclic Synchronous Torque mode (CST)


  

4: Open-loop speed (Inverter drive)


  

5: Reference Encoder


  

6: Open loop position (Stepper drive)
examples:
'Setup 4 axes in MC_CONFIG


'Note: ATYPE is set automatically, do not set in MC_CONFIG


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(0)=1 ' position mode


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(1)=1 ' position mode


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(2)=2 ' velocity mode


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(3)=3 ' torque mode
'Setup 2 axes in MC_CONFIG


'Note: ATYPE is set automatically, do not set in MC_CONFIG


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(0)=6 ' position mode with no feedback


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(1)=6 ' position mode with no feedback

'Setup 4 axes in MC_CONFIG


'Note: ATYPE is set automatically, do not set in MC_CONFIG


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(0)=1 ' position mode


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(1)=1 ' position mode


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(2)=2 ' velocity mode


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(3)=3 ' torque mode

'Setup 2 axes in MC_CONFIG


'Note: ATYPE is set automatically, do not set in MC_CONFIG


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(0)=6 ' position mode with no feedback


DRIVE_MODE AXIS(1)=6 ' position mode with no feedback

name: DRIVE_MONITOR
description: Returns current value of parameter identified using DRIVE_CONTROL.
examples:
DRIVE_CONTROL = 5
fe_actual = DRIVE_MONITOR

name: DRIVE_NEG_TORQUE
description: Sets the negative torque limit value in a remote drive. For this value to be active, the cyclic data transfer from the drive must be first configured to set the NEGATIVE_TORQUE_LIMIT value. For a drive connected by CANopen over EtherCAT (CoE) the value will be configured as part of the Process Data Object (PDO). Usually, the parameter is mapped to Object 0x60E1 sub index 0x00, size 16 bits.
examples: DRIVE_NEG_TORQUE AXIS(2) = 500

name: DRIVE_PARAMETER
syntax:
DRIVE_PARAMETER AXIS(n) = value


 


value = DRIVE_PARAMETER AXIS(n)
description: This parameter is a general name that is used to allow access to some named objects via the EtherCAT PDO. DRIVE_PARAMETER may be mapped to either TxPDO or RxPDO objects by using the EC_EXTEND EtherCAT Extension file. EC_EXTEND RxPDO containing TARGET_MAX_CURRENT The parameter in the PDO shown here will be mapped to DRIVE_PARAMETER. EC_EXTEND SDO initialisation list. (Pre-Op -> Safe-Op) The TARGET_MAX_CURRENT, being the 3 rd object in the PDO, will be mapped to drive object 0x6073, sub-index 0x00 as shown.

name: DRIVE_POS_TORQUE
description: Sets the positive torque limit value in a remote drive. For this value to be active, the cyclic data transfer from the drive must be first configured to set the POSITIVE_TORQUE_LIMIT value. For a drive connected by CANopen over EtherCAT (CoE) the value will be configured as part of the Process Data Object (PDO). Usually, the parameter is mapped to Object 0x60E0 sub index 0x00, size 16 bits.
examples:
DRIVE_POS_TORQUE AXIS(2) = 100
BASE(1)
DRIVE_POS_TORQUE = 1000
MOVE(30)


WAIT UNTIL REMAIN < 4
DRIVE_POS_TORQUE = 50

name: DRIVE_PROFILE
description: DRIVE_PROFILE allows the selection of different EtherCAT profiles from the internal database to be used with a remote drive over EtherCAT. This MUST be set in MC_CONFIG if the EtherCAT is to be initialised on power up with the required profile. This axis parameter can be added to the MC_CONFIG. The EtherCAT drive must have an application specific profile within the Motion Coordinator’s internal database before this function can be used.
params:
Use the default “standard profile” with minimum objects
  passed between drive and Motion Coordinator.
Pulse and Direction output with Z input (registration).

Square wave and Direction output with Z input
  (registration)

Pulse and Direction counter with Z input
  (registration).
examples:
DRIVE_PROFILE AXIS(0)=2


DRIVE_PROFILE AXIS(1)=2


DRIVE_PROFILE AXIS(2)=2


DRIVE_PROFILE AXIS(3)=1

DRIVE_PROFILE AXIS(0)=2


DRIVE_PROFILE AXIS(1)=2


DRIVE_PROFILE AXIS(2)=2


DRIVE_PROFILE AXIS(3)=1

name: DRIVE_READ
description: DRIVE_READ allows the controller to read a parameter from a digital bus connected drive. Currently this is supports Panasonic A4N, A5N and A6N drives. The parameter index and details can be found in the Motion Perfect intelligent drives tool.
params:
If vr_index is not used the return value is the
  parameter value

Read a System ID into a 
VRSTRING

VR
 in which to store the returned
  value
Index of alarm to be read
examples:
success = DRIVE_READ(124,0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error reading drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "External scale direction = "; VR(0)[0]
ENDIF
success = DRIVE_READ(3 * 256 + 26,0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error reading drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "External scale direction = "; VR(0)[0]
ENDIF
success = DRIVE_READ($10000 + $130,0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error reading drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "Driver Serial No. = ";VRSTRING(0)
ENDIF
PRINT "Alarm Read
AXIS(";axis_no[0];")"
FOR past_alarm = 0 TO 14
DRIVE_READ($20000 + past_alarm * 4096 + 0 ,0)
PRINT "Alarm history index ";
past_alarm[0];" = ";VR(0)[0]
NEXT past_alarm
success = DRIVE_READ($30000 + $102, 0)
IF success = FALSE THEN
PRINT "Error reading drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "Encoder resolution = ";VR(0)[0]
ENDIF
pos = DRIVE_READ($30201)
oneturn=10000' Distance for one turn depends on encoder
type
IF pos <> -1 THEN
PRINT "Mechanical offset:";pos[0]
ELSE
PRINT "Drive has not yet seen Z mark"
MOVE(oneturn)
WAIT UNTIL DRIVE_READ($30201)<>-1
CANCEL
WAIT IDLE
pos = DRIVE_READ($30201)
PRINT "Mechanical offset:";pos[0]
ENDIF
DEFPOS(pos)

success = DRIVE_READ(124,0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error reading drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "External scale direction = "; VR(0)[0]
ENDIF

success = DRIVE_READ(3 * 256 + 26,0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error reading drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "External scale direction = "; VR(0)[0]
ENDIF

success = DRIVE_READ($10000 + $130,0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error reading drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "Driver Serial No. = ";VRSTRING(0)
ENDIF

PRINT "Alarm Read
AXIS(";axis_no[0];")"
FOR past_alarm = 0 TO 14
DRIVE_READ($20000 + past_alarm * 4096 + 0 ,0)
PRINT "Alarm history index ";
past_alarm[0];" = ";VR(0)[0]
NEXT past_alarm

success = DRIVE_READ($30000 + $102, 0)
IF success = FALSE THEN
PRINT "Error reading drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "Encoder resolution = ";VR(0)[0]
ENDIF

pos = DRIVE_READ($30201)
oneturn=10000' Distance for one turn depends on encoder
type
IF pos <> -1 THEN
PRINT "Mechanical offset:";pos[0]
ELSE
PRINT "Drive has not yet seen Z mark"
MOVE(oneturn)
WAIT UNTIL DRIVE_READ($30201)<>-1
CANCEL
WAIT IDLE
pos = DRIVE_READ($30201)
PRINT "Mechanical offset:";pos[0]
ENDIF
DEFPOS(pos)

name: DRIVE_REP_DIST
description: Sets the wrap-around value that the axis is expecting from an EtherCAT or RTEX servodrive. The default axis roll-over is at 2 32 as this is the data size for cyclic position transfers over the fieldbus, but if the drive or encoder wrap at a lower number than this, it will create an axis error if the Motion Coordinator is expecting the axis to continue counting up. The DRIVE_REP_DIST should be set in cases where the motor has a multi-turn absolute encoder fitted and the drive has been set up to roll-over to 0 at some position lower than 2 32. The value of DRIVE_REP_DIST must be the same as the position roll-over that is set in the drive or fieldbus encoder. This parameter enables the axis in the controller to coordinate the actual position reset to 0 with the remote device. Do not use DRIVE_REP_DIST when the actual position value sent in the cyclic data from the drive or encoder counts up to the full 32 bit value. Check the drive manual and settings.
examples:
BASE(4)


' One turn 2^23 = 8388608


' 10 turns = 83886080


DRIVE_REP_DIST = 83886080
' Now set up units to scale the axis in degrees of the


' turntable.
UNITS = 83886080 / 360





' Set the axis repeat distance and mode to match the


' position roll-over. (these parameters affect MPOS)


REP_DIST = 360


REP_OPTION = 1

name: DRIVE_SET_VAL
description: Internal parameter used for RTEX and SLM axes.

name: DRIVE_STATUS
description: Returns the Status Word received cyclically from a remote drive connected by a fieldbus. For example in CANopen over EtherCAT (CoE) the DRIVE_STATUS would have the value from object $6041 sub-index $00.
examples: PRINT #5,HEX(DRIVE_STATUS AXIS(4))

name: DRIVE_TORQUE
description: Returns the actual torque value calculated by a remote drive. For this value to be active, the cyclic data transfer from the drive must be first configured to return the drive actual torque value. For a drive connected by CANopen over EtherCAT (CoE) the value will be configured as part of the Process Data Object (PDO).
examples:
PRINT #5,"Drive torque value = "; DRIVE_TORQUE
AXIS(2)
BASE(16)
WAIT UNTIL DRIVE_TORQUE < 3000

name: DRIVE_TYPE
description: Reserved Keyword

name: DRIVE_VALUE
description: Internal parameter used on RTEX and SLM axes.

name: DRIVE_VELOCITY
description: Returns the actual velocity value from a remote drive. For this value to be active, the cyclic data transfer from the drive must be first configured to return the drive actual velocity value. For a drive connected by CANOpen over EtherCAT (CoE) the value will be configured as part of the Process Data Object. (PDO)
examples:
PRINT #5,"Drive current value = ";DRIVE_VELOCITY
AXIS(2)
BASE(16)
WAIT UNTIL DRIVE_VELOCITY < 500

name: DRIVE_WRITE
description: DRIVE_WRITE allows the controller to write to a parameter from a digital bus connected drive. Currently this is supports Panasonic A4N, A5N and A6N RTEX drives. The parameter numbers and details can be found in the Motion Perfect intelligent drives tool.
params: Stores all drive parameters into EPROM
Resets all drive parameters to default values

The value to be written to the parameter.


  

(Use 0 for parameter numbers 128 & 129)
examples:
success = DRIVE_WRITE(122, 10000)
If success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error writing drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "Encoder scale set"
ENDIF
success = DRIVE_WRITE(0 * 256 + 8, 15000)
If success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error writing drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "Encoder scale set"
ENDIF
success = DRIVE_WRITE(128, 0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error storing drive parameters to
EPROM"
ELSE
PRINT "Drive parameters stored in EPROM"
ENDIF
success = DRIVE_WRITE(129, 0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error resetting drive parameters"
ELSE
PRINT "Drive parameters reset to defaults"
ENDIF

success = DRIVE_WRITE(122, 10000)
If success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error writing drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "Encoder scale set"
ENDIF

success = DRIVE_WRITE(0 * 256 + 8, 15000)
If success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error writing drive parameter"
ELSE
PRINT "Encoder scale set"
ENDIF

success = DRIVE_WRITE(128, 0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error storing drive parameters to
EPROM"
ELSE
PRINT "Drive parameters stored in EPROM"
ENDIF

success = DRIVE_WRITE(129, 0)
IF success = 0 THEN
PRINT "Error resetting drive parameters"
ELSE
PRINT "Drive parameters reset to defaults"
ENDIF

name: DUMP
description: For internal debug use by the manufacturer only. The DUMP command outputs a memory image to terminal 0.

name: DV_CONTROLWORD
description: Sets up the operation of a remote EtherCAT Flexslice device such as the thermocouple module or RTD module.
params:
The value read from or written to the device variable’s
  control word

name: DV_IN
description: DV_IN returns the value, known as a Device Variable, from a remote I/O block such as the Flexslice temperature modules, thermocouple and RTD type. During system startup the channels are allocated according to the DV_MAP, usually there will be 4 or 8 channels per module.
params: The value read from the device’s input
examples:
DIM temperature_ch0, temperature_ch1, temperature_ch2,_
temperature_ch3 AS FLOAT
DIM data_index AS INTEGER = 0
' read 4 x temperature values from P367
temperature_ch0 = DV_IN(data_index) / 100
temperature_ch1 = DV_IN(data_index + 1) / 100
temperature_ch2 = DV_IN(data_index + 2) / 100
temperature_ch3 = DV_IN(data_index + 3) / 100
' Compare with setpoints, set output channel to turn on
heater
IF temperature_ch0 < sp1 THEN
DV_OUT(data_index) = 1
ELSE
DV_OUT(data_index) = 0
ENDIF
IF temperature_ch1 < sp2 THEN
DV_OUT(data_index + 1) = 1
ELSE
DV_OUT(data_index + 1) = 0
ENDIF
IF temperature_ch2 < sp3 THEN
DV_OUT(data_index + 2) = 1
ELSE
DV_OUT(data_index + 2) = 0
ENDIF
IF temperature_ch3 < sp4 THEN
DV_OUT(data_index + 3) = 1
ELSE
DV_OUT(data_index + 3) = 0
ENDIF

name: DV_MAP
description: Displays the current DV_IN and DV_OUT mapping to the command line terminal. Open terminal #0 and type DV_MAP at the >> prompt.
examples:
>>DV_MAP
Device Variable map :


DV_IN map :
0-  3 : ECAT Input Module @ Slot 0 (Auto Inc Address
= 1)
4-  7 : ECAT Input Module @ Slot 0 (Auto Inc Address
= 2)
DV_OUT map :
0-  3 : ECAT Output Module @ Slot 0 (Auto Inc Address
= 1)
4-  7 : ECAT Output Module @ Slot 0 (Auto Inc Address
= 2)

name: DV_OUT
description: DV_OUT sets the state or value, known as a Device Variable, on a remote I/O block such as the Flexslice temperature modules, thermocouple and RTD type. During system startup the channels are allocated according to the DV_MAP, usually there will be 4 or 8 channels per module. DV_OUT also returns the current state or value of the Device Variable.
params:
The integer value read from or written to the device
  variable’s output
examples:
DIM temperature AS FLOAT
DIM channel_number AS INTEGER = 2
DIM p_cycle AS INTEGER = 500 ' half second cycle time
DIM p_mark AS INTEGER ' mark/space value as time in msecs
DIM setpoint AS FLOAT
TICKS = 0 ' TICKS counts down one count per servo cycle
WHILE TRUE
' read temperature value from P367
temperature = DV_IN(channel_number) / 100
setpoint = VR(0) ' degrees celcius
' Compare with setpoint to produce a temperature error
value
temperature_error = setpoint - temperature
loop_gain = VR(1) ' typical values 1 .. 10
p_mark = temperature_error * loop_gain * p_cycle / 100
IF TICKS > -p_mark THEN
DV_OUT(channel_number) = 1
ELSE
DV_OUT(channel_number) = 0
ENDIF
IF TICKS < -p_cycle THEN
TICKS = 0
ENDIF
WEND

name: DV_STATUSWORD
description: Returns the status word from a remote Flexslice EtherCAT module such as the thermocouple module and RTD module.
params: The value read from the device variable’s status word

name: D_GAIN
description: Used as part of the closed loop control, adding derivative gain to a system is likely to produce a smoother response and allow the use of a higher proportional gain than could otherwise be used. High values may lead to oscillation. For a derivative term K d and a change in following error de the contribution to the output O d signal is: O d = K d × δ e
examples:
P_GAIN=1
I_GAIN=0
D_GAIN=0.25
OV_GAIN=0
…

name: D_ZONE_MAX
description: Working in conjunction with D_ZONE_MIN , D_ZONE_MAX defines a DAC dead band. This clamps the DAC output to zero when the demand movement is complete and the magnitude of the following error is less than the D_ ZONE _MIN value. The servo loop will be reactivated when either the following error rises above the D_ZONE_MAX value, or a fresh movement is started. This can be used to prevent oscillations at static positions in Piezo systems.
examples:
D_ZONE_MIN = 3
D_ZONE_MAX = 10

name: D_ZONE_MIN
description: Working in conjunction with D_ZONE_MAX , D_ZONE_MIN defines a DAC dead band. This clamps the DAC output to zero when the demand movement is complete and the magnitude of the following error is less than the D_ZONE_MIN value. The servo loop will be reactivated when either the following error rises above the D_ZONE_MAX value, or a fresh movement is started. This can be used to prevent oscillations at static positions in Piezo systems.
examples:
D_ZONE_MIN = 3
D_ZONE_MAX = 10

name: /  Divide
description: Divides expression1 by expression2

name: $ Dollar
description: The $ symbol is used to specify that the following signed 53 bit number (for floating point) or 63 bit (for integer) is in hexadecimal format using the digits 0-9 and A-F.
examples: OP($CC00)

name: ECAT_MODE
description: ECAT_MODE sets various actions within the EtherCAT part of the firmware. Each bit in ECAT_MODE is used to turn on or off the feature described below. The parameter can be set in MC_CONFIG, used on the command line or in a program. The mode set will be active immediately after the parameter has been written.
params:
The slot which is running EtherCAT.  


  

In the MC664(X) slot_number is the physical slot of the
  EtherCAT module if fitted.  The built in EtherCAT port has the fixed
  slot_number -2.


  

IN the MC4N-ECAT, MC6N-ECAT and Flex-6 Nano, Flex-6X
  Nano, the slot_number is 0.

Emergency message response.  The EtherCAT protocol
  raises a SYSTEM_ERROR (0x100000) when an emergency message is received from a
  slave and the controller logs the error.

The EtherCAT logs the error but no SYSTEM_ERROR is
  raised.

Standard EtherCAT start-up.  The EtherCAT protocol waits
  until all the sync0 start-up times have expired before requesting the Safe Op
  to Operational state change. By default ( bit 1 = 0 )

Express EtherCAT start-up.  The EtherCAT protocol does
  not wait for all the sync0 start-up times to expire.  It requests the Safe Op
  to Operational state change at the earliest time.


  

Note that some slaves might require the user program to
  clear slave errors after the network is in Operational state.
examples: ECAT_MODE SLOT(0) = 1

name: EDPROG
description: This is a special command that may be used to manipulate the SELECT ed programs on the controller. It is not normally used except by Motion Perfect.

name: EDPROG1
description: This is a special command that may be used to manipulate the SELECT ed programs on the controller. It is not normally used except by Motion Perfect.

name: ENCODER_CONTROL
description: Endat encoders can be set to either cyclically return their position, or they can be set to a parameter read/write mode. Using the ENCODER_READ or ENCODER_WRITE functions will set the parameter to 1 automatically.
examples:
value = ENCODER_READ($A700)
ENCODER_CONTROL = 0

name: ENCODER_FILTER
description: This parameter allows filtering to be applied to an encoder feedback to reduce the impact of jitter. The smaller the value the larger the time constant and so the less impact jitter will have on the system. This parameter can be used to reduce jitter on a master axis which is linked to another axis.
examples:
BASE(0)
ENCODER_FILTER = 0.95
BASE(1)
CONNECT(1,0)

name: ENCODER_ID
description: This parameter returns the Encoder Identification (ENID) parameter from a Tamagawa absolute encoder.
examples:
ATYPE = 46
IF ENCODER_ID<>17 THEN
PRINT#term, “Incorrect ENID”
ENDIF

name: ENCODER_RATIO
description: This command allows the incoming encoder count to be scaled by a non integer ratio: MPOS = (mpos_count / input_count) x encoder_edges_input When using the servo loop you will need to adjust the gains to maintain performance and stability. Unlike the UNITS parameter, which only affects the scaling seen by the user programs, ENCODER_RATIO affects all motion commands. ENCODER_RATIO does not replace UNITS . Only use ENCODER_RATIO where absolutely necessary. PP_STEP and ENCODER_RATIO cannot be used at the same time on the same axis.
params: An integer number which defines the numerator
An integer number which defines the denominator
examples:
ENCODER_RATIO(7200,8192)
UNITS = 20 ‘ axis calibrated in degrees
ENCODER_RATIO(560,409) AXIS(3) 'axis 3 is now 560
counts/mm
UNITS AXIS(3) = 56 'X axis calibrated in mm x 10
UNTIS AXIS(4) = 56 'Y axis calibrated in mm x 10
MOVECIRC(200,100,100,0,1) 'move axes in a semicircle
BASE(5) ' set axis 5 to work in reverse direction
DAC_SCALE = -1
ENCODER_RATIO(-1,1)
BASE(30) ' set axis 30 to work in reverse direction
ENCODER_RATIO(-1,1)
STEP_RATIO(-1,1)

ENCODER_RATIO(7200,8192)
UNITS = 20 ‘ axis calibrated in degrees

ENCODER_RATIO(560,409) AXIS(3) 'axis 3 is now 560
counts/mm
UNITS AXIS(3) = 56 'X axis calibrated in mm x 10
UNTIS AXIS(4) = 56 'Y axis calibrated in mm x 10
MOVECIRC(200,100,100,0,1) 'move axes in a semicircle

BASE(5) ' set axis 5 to work in reverse direction
DAC_SCALE = -1
ENCODER_RATIO(-1,1)

BASE(30) ' set axis 30 to work in reverse direction
ENCODER_RATIO(-1,1)
STEP_RATIO(-1,1)

name: ENCODER_READ
description: Read an internal register from an EnDat absolute encoder.
params:
Value returned from the specified register. Returns -1
  if the encoder has not been initialised
The address of the EnDat encoder register to be read
examples:
ENCODER_BITS=25+256*12
ATYPE=47
IF ENCODER_READ($A700)=-1 then
PRINT “Failed to initialise EnDat Encoder
ENDIF
ENCODER_CONTROL=0
>>BASE(1)
>>PRINT ENCODER_READ($A10d)AND $3F
25
>>

name: ENCODER_STATUS
description: This axis parameter returns both the status field SF and the ALMC encoder error field from a Tamagawa absolute encoder.
examples:
PRINT "SF field = "; HEX(ENCODER_STATUS AND
$FF)
PRINT "ALMC field = "; HEX((ENCODER_STATUS AND
$FF00) / $FF)

name: ENCODER_TURNS
description: Returns the number of multi-turn counts from EnDat or Tamagawa absolute encoders. The multi-turn data is not automatically applied to the axis MPOS after initialisation of a Tamagawa absolute encoder. The application programmer must apply this from BASIC using OFFPOS or DEFPOS as required.
examples:
ATYPE=46
OFFPOS= ENCODER_TURNS*2^17
WAIT UNTIL OFFPOS = 0

name: ENCODER_WRITE
description: Write an internal register to an Absolute Encoder on an EnDat absolute encoder.
params:
Returns 
TRUE
 if the write was
  successful and 
FALSE
 if it fails

The address of the EnDat encoder register to be written
  to
Value to be written to the specified register.
examples:
IF NOT ENCODER_WRITE (endat_address, setvalue) THEN
PRINT “Fail to write to encoder”
ENDIF
ENCODER_CONTROL=0

name: ENDMOVE
description: This parameter holds the absolute position of the end of the current move in user units. It is normally only read back although may be written to if required provided that SERVO = ON and no move is in progress. Writing to DPOS will make a step changes. This can easily lead to “Following error exceeds limit” errors unless the steps are small or the FE_LIMIT is high. As it is an absolute value ENDMOVE is adjusted by OFFPOS / DEFPOS . The individual moves in the buffer are incremental and are not adjusted by OFFPOS .
examples:
MOVE(distance*pitch)
IF ENDMOVE>200 THEN
CANCEL
PRINT#5, “Calculated distance to large”
ENDIF

name: ENDMOVE_SPEED
description: This parameter sets the end speed for a motion command that supports advanced speed control (commands ending in SP). Subject to the AXIS_BLENDING mode, the VP_SPEED will decelerate so that ENDMOVE_SPEED is reached at the end of the motion command. ENDMOVE_SPEED is loaded into the buffer at the same time as the move so you can set different speeds for subsequent moves. If there are no further motion commands in the buffer the current move will decelerate to a stop. ENDMOVE_SPEED has a default value of -1 to disable its use.
examples:
FORCE_SPEED = 15
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 10
MOVESP(20)
FORCE_SPEED = 10
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 5
MOVESP(5)
FORCE_SPEED = 1000
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 500 '50% of FORCE_SPEED
MOVESP(100, 10)
MOVESP(70, -10)
MOVESP(120, 15)

FORCE_SPEED = 15
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 10
MOVESP(20)
FORCE_SPEED = 10
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 5
MOVESP(5)

FORCE_SPEED = 1000
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 500 '50% of FORCE_SPEED
MOVESP(100, 10)
MOVESP(70, -10)
MOVESP(120, 15)

name: END_DIR_LAST
description: Returns the direction in radians of the end of the last loaded advanced speed control interpolated motion command (those suffixed “SP”). The direction is that of the first two axes of the move loaded, the direction being relative to the Y axis, so 0 is up the Y axis, PI/2 is up X axis, +PI and -PI are down the Y axis, and -PI/2 is down the X axis. You can use the parameter to set an initial direction before loading a SP motion command. Write to END_DIR_LAST of the base axis when initialising a system or after a sequence of moves which are not SP commands. Note that this parameter is relevant to SP motion commands only, such as MOVESP , MOVEABSSP etc. Note that as this parameter is an angle, it is primarily intended for 2D direction mode, and if used when 3D direction mode is enabled will set the direction of the first two axes only (direction mode is set with AXIS_MODE bit 2), i.e. there will be no Z component.
examples:
>>MOVESP(10000, -10000)
>>PRINT END_DIR_LAST
2.3562
>>
MOVESP(100, 100)


PRINT "End 1 = "; END_DIR_LAST * 180 / PI


MOVESP(100, 0)


PRINT "Change 1 = "; CHANGE_DIR_LAST * 180 / PI


WAIT IDLE





MOVESP(100, 100)


END_DIR_LAST = 40 * PI / 180 'Modify the previous move's end direction


MOVESP(100, 0)


PRINT "Change 2 = "; CHANGE_DIR_LAST * 180 / PI


WAIT IDLE
End 1 = 45.0000


Change 1 = 45.0000


Change 2 = 40.0000

>>MOVESP(10000, -10000)
>>PRINT END_DIR_LAST
2.3562
>>

MOVESP(100, 100)


PRINT "End 1 = "; END_DIR_LAST * 180 / PI


MOVESP(100, 0)


PRINT "Change 1 = "; CHANGE_DIR_LAST * 180 / PI


WAIT IDLE





MOVESP(100, 100)


END_DIR_LAST = 40 * PI / 180 'Modify the previous move's end direction


MOVESP(100, 0)


PRINT "Change 2 = "; CHANGE_DIR_LAST * 180 / PI


WAIT IDLE
End 1 = 45.0000


Change 1 = 45.0000


Change 2 = 40.0000

name: EPROM
description: This parameter is included in the token list to allow older programs to compile in the MC4xx/MC5xx/MC6xx Motion Coordinators. It does not have a function because all programs are automatically stored in the Flash EPROM and so are never contained only in the RAM.
examples:
>>EPROM(0)
Erasing Application area of FLASH
Programming
Verifying

name: EPROM_STATUS
description: Returns the status of background FLASH memory programming. 0 Not active 1 Flash memory is being Erased 2 Erasing is suspended Flash memory programming takes place when programs are loaded or edited. Also when a VR value is changed and when TABLE values are changed in Motion Coordinators that support automatic TABLE store to Flash. As each Flash page becomes full, a new page is erased ready to continue programming. This parameter can be checked before powering down the Motion Coordinator, however when the power is removed, a power-off detection will finalise any auto-store that is in progress in the time available before the processor shuts down. In normal use it is not necessary to wait for the FLASH_STATUS to show 0 before powering down. Motion Perfect shows the FLASH_STATUS as an indicator marked “Flash”.

name: ERRORMASK
description: The value held in this parameter is bitwise AND ed with the AXISSTATUS parameter by every axis on every servo cycle to determine if a runtime error should switch off the enable ( WDOG ) relay. If the result of the AND operation is not zero the enable relay is switched off. After a critical error has tripped the enable relay, the Motion Coordinator must either be reset, or a DATUM (0) command must be executed to reset the error flags.

name: ERROR_AXIS
description: Returns the number of the axis that caused the MOTION_ERROR . ERROR_AXIS should only be read when MOTION_ERROR <> 0
examples:
IF MOTION_ERROR THEN
PRINT#5, "Axis to cause error = "; ERROR_AXIS
PRINT#5, "AXISSTATUS of ERROR_AXIS
= "; AXISSTATUS AXIS(ERROR_AXIS)
ENDIF

name: ERROR_LINE
description: Stores the number of the line which caused the last Trio BASIC error. This value is only valid when the BASICERROR is TRUE . This parameter is held independently for each process.
examples:
error_routine:
VR(100) = RUN_ERROR
PRINT "The error "; RUN_ERROR[0];
PRINT " occurred in line "; ERROR_LINE[0]
STOP

name: ETHERCAT
description: The command ETHERCAT is used to perform advanced operations on the EtherCAT network. In normal use the EtherCAT network will start automatically without the need for any commands in a startup program. Some ETHERCAT command functions may be useful when debugging and setting up an EtherCAT system, so a small sub-set is described here. The ETHERCAT command returns TRUE (-1) if successful and FALSE (0) if the command execution was in error. Functions which return a value must either put the value in a VR or print it to the current output terminal.
params: Send reset sequence to a drive
Set Status Mask and Trigger Pattern
Get Status Mask and Trigger Pattern
Set to the EtherCAT port slot number

Internal slot number or -2


  

P876 slot number if fitted

name: ETHERNET
description: The command ETHERNET is used to configure the operation of the Ethernet port. Many of the ETHERNET functions are command line only; these are stored in flash EPROM and are then used on power up.
params: Set to -1 for the built in Ethernet port
Configure endpoints for Modbus TCP or Ethernet IP
Set the maximum packets handled per stack call
List status of ports to the terminal
Reserved for device specific PHY registers
The first byte of the IP address
The second byte of the IP address
The third byte of the IP address
The fourth byte of the IP address
The first byte of the Subnet Mask
The second byte of the Subnet Mask
The third byte of the Subnet Mask
The fourth byte of the Subnet Mask

The port used for the main command line in the
  controller. (default 23)

The port used for the token channel in the controller.
  (default 3240)

32-bit single precision floating point without address
  halving
32-bit long word integers without address halving
The first byte of the Default Gateway
The second byte of the Default Gateway
The third byte of the Default Gateway
The fourth byte of the Default Gateway

The port used for the token channel in the controller.
  (default 502)

This allows you to specify which end point you are
  reading or writing

This parameter selects which of the endpoint variables
  you are reading or writing
Dependent on Parameter index, see table below
The start position of the data location.
The location of the data on the controller.
The number of the data locations returned.
The class. This function is read only.
The instance of the endpoint. This function is read only.

Set memory mapping for Modbus TCP.


  

32-bit values will have automatic address halving.

Modbus
  request address. If request address of -1 is used, then all defined regions
  are reset

Base
  address on the controller memory area to which the request address is mapped

Number
  of Modbus registers in the mapped region. Minimum value is 1.

  

Given
  that this function automatically applies address halving, the number of
  mapped controller memory registers will depend on the data type.

  

For
  16-bit data types then each Modbus register is mapped to one controller value
  so the mapped address area is from the base address to (base address +
  num_entries – 1).

  

For
  32-bit data types then each Modbus register is mapped to two controller
  values so the mapped address area is from the base address to (base address +
  (num_entries/2) – 1).

name: ETHERNETIP
description: This parameter is included in the command list to allow older programs to compile in the MC4xx/MC5xx/MC6xx Motion Coordinators. It does not have a function in the current firmware. For Ethernet IP setup, see ETHERNET function 14.

name: EX
description: Software reset. Resets the controller as if it were being powered up. When performing an EX on the command line you will see the controller start up information that provides details of your controller configuration. On EX the following actions occur: The global numbered ( VR ) variables remain in memory. The base axis array is reset to 0,1,2... on all processes Axis errors are cleared Watchdog is set OFF Programs may be run depending on POWER_UP and RUNTYPE settings ALL axis parameters are reset. EX may be included in a program. This can be useful following a run time error. Care must be taken to ensure it is safe to restart the program. When running Motion Perfect executing an EX command is not allowed. The same effect as an EX can be obtained by using “Reset the controller...” under the “Controller” menu in Motion Perfect. To simply re-start the programs, use the AUTORUN command.

name: EXECUTE
description: Used to implement the remote command execution via the Trio PCMotion ActiveX. For more details see the section on using the PCMotion

name: EXIT_LOOP
description: This command applies to the loop structures WHILE…WEND, FOR TO…NEXT and REPEAT…UNTIL. Once this command is executed within one of the structures mentioned above, the execution will jump out of the loop, therefore, the following iterations will never be performed.
examples:
WHILE IN(12) = OFF
MOVE(200)
WAIT IDLE
IF IN(9) = ON THEN
EXIT_LOOP
ENDIF
OP(10, OFF)
MOVE(-200)
WAIT IDLE
OP(10, ON)
WEND

name: EXP
description: Returns the exponential value of the expression.

name: FALSE
description: The constant FALSE takes the numerical value of 0.
examples:
test:
res = IN(0) OR IN(2)
IF res = FALSE THEN
PRINT "Inputs are off"
ENDIF

name: FASTDEC
description: The FASTDEC axis parameter may be used to set or read back the fast deceleration rate of each axis fitted. The fast deceleration and fast jerk rates are used when motion is cancelled, either via the CANCEL or RAPIDSTOP commands, or from a software or hardware limit. FASTDECEL must be greater than DECEL, otherwise DECEL will be used.

name: FASTJERK
description: The FASTJERK axis parameter may be used to set or read the fast jerk rate of each axis fitted. The fast deceleration and fast jerk rates are used when motion is cancelled, either via the CANCEL or RAPIDSTOP commands, or from a software or hardware limit. FASTJERK is applied only when using VP_MODE 5 or 6. FASTJERK must be greater than JERK, otherwise JERK will be used. As of firmware version 2.0342, the SRAMP keyword may be used instead of FASTJERK, but if SRAMP is assigned a value (even 0), then any assignment to FASTJERK will be ignored. When SRAMP is used, the FASTJERK is calculated from FASTDEC / SRAMP. Using SRAMP with VP_MODE = 6 is intended solely to aid portability for existing applications, and it is strongly recommended that new applications use JERK and FASTJERK.

name: FAST_JOG
description: This parameter holds the input number to be used as the fast jog input. If the FAST_JOG is active then the jog inputs use the axis SPEED for the jog functions, otherwise the JOGSPEED will be used. The input used for FAST_JOG is active low.
examples:
FWD_JOG = 12
FAST_JOG = 13
JOGSPEED = 200

name: FE
description: This parameter returns the position error, which is equal to the demand position ( DPOS ) - measured position ( MPOS ).
examples:
MOVEABS(200)
WAIT IDLE
FOR x=0 to 4
WAIT UNTIL FE<5
NEXT x
OP(5,ON)
WA(2)
OP(5,OFF)

name: FEATURE_ENABLE
description: Motion Coordinators can unlock additional features by entering a “Feature Enable Code”, or FEC. This function is used to enable protected features, such as additional remote axes on digital dive networks or other programming languages. This can only be run on the command line. It is recommended to use Motion Perfect to enter and store the feature enable codes. The password parameter is optional, if it is omitted then the command will prompt you to enter it. You can purchase additional feature codes from the Trio Website or through your distributor, you will need the SERIAL_NUMBER of the controller. If you enter the wrong password 3 times the controller will enter an attack state where it stops communicating. You can resume normal operation by power cycling the controller.
params:
Prints the security code and currently enabled
  features.
The password for the required feature code
examples:
>>FEATURE_ENABLE
Security code=17980000000028
Enabled features: 0 1
>>FEATURE_ENABLE(2)
Feature 2 Password=5P0APT
>>
>>FEATURE_ENABLE
Security code=17980000000028
Enabled features: 0 1 2

>>FEATURE_ENABLE
Security code=17980000000028
Enabled features: 0 1

>>FEATURE_ENABLE(2)
Feature 2 Password=5P0APT
>>
>>FEATURE_ENABLE
Security code=17980000000028
Enabled features: 0 1 2

name: FEED_OVERRIDE
description: This parameter can be used to override the feed rate when using an embedded speed profile such as MOVECONTOUR. The default value is 100%, and a value between 0% and 150% can be set. The path speed (feed-rate) is ramped up or down to the new value.
examples:
BASE(3) 'Control the feed rate on moves with base axis 3
WHILE TRUE
feed_rate = AIN(1) * 150 / 2048
IF feed_rate < 0 THEN feed_rate = 0
FEED_OVERRIDE = feed_rate
WA(100)
WEND

name: FE_LATCH
description: Contains the FE value which caused the axis to put the controller into MOTION_ERROR . This value is only set when the FE exceeds the FE_LIMIT and the SERVO = OFF .
examples:
IF MOTION_ERROR THEN
VR(10) = FE_LATCH AXIS(ERROR_AXIS)
ENDIF

name: FE_LIMIT
description: This is the maximum allowable following error. When exceeded the controller will generate an AXISSTATUS error, by default this will also generate a MOTION_ERROR . The MOTION_ERROR will disable the WDOG relay thus stopping further motor operation. This limit may be used to guard against fault conditions such as mechanical lock-up, loss of encoder feedback, etc.
examples:
FOR x = 0 to 4
BASE(x)
UNITS = 100
FE_LIMIT = 10
SPEED = 100
ACCEL = 1000
DECEL = ACCEL
NEXT x

name: FE_LIMIT_MODE
description: This parameter determines if an AXISSTATUS error is produced immediately when the FE exceeds the FE_LIMIT or if it exceeds for 2 consecutive servo periods. This means that if FE_LIMIT is exceeded for one servo period only, it will be ignored. This will increase the time to disable your drives in an error. You should only change from the default values under advice from Trio or your distributor.

name: FE_RANGE
description: Following error report range. When the FE exceeds this value, the axis has bit 1 in the AXISSTATUS axis parameter set.
examples:
'Initialise the axis
FE_RANGE = 10
FE_LIMIT = 15
SPEED = 100
'loop to check if FE_RANGE has been exceeded
WHILE NOT IDLE
VR(10) = AXISSTATUS
IF READBIT(1, 10) THEN
'Slow down by 1%
SPEED = SPEED * 0.99
ENDIF
WEND
SPEED = 100

name: FHOLD_IN
description: This parameter specifies the input number to be used as the feed-hold input. When the feed-hold input is active, motion on the specified axis has its speed overridden to the feed-hold speed ( FHSPEED ) without cancelling the move in progress. The change in speed uses ACCEL , DECEL and JERK . When the input is reset, any move in progress when the input was set will revert to its programmed speed. The default setting of FHSPEED is zero, which will cause motion to pause on that axis Moves that are not speed controlled, such as CONNECT , CAMBOX , MOVELINK , are not affected. The input used for FHOLD_IN is active low.
examples:
INVERT_IN
(
32
,
 1
) 


FHOLD_IN = 
32


FHSPEED = 
0



WAIT
 UNTIL
 IN
(
32
) = 
OFF



BASE
(
0
)


ATYPE = 
0


SPEED = 
10000


ACCEL = 
1000000


DECEL = ACCEL



MOVE
(
999999
)

name: FHSPEED
description: The feed-hold speed defines the speed to use when the feed-hold input ( FHOLD_IN ) is active.
examples:
BASE(3)
SPEED = 1000
FHSPEED = 0.1 * SPEED

name: FIFO_READ
description: See OPEN for details.

name: FIFO_WRITE
description: See OPEN for details.

name: FILE
description: This command enables the user to manage the data on the SD Card. When the command prints to the selected channel, this channel can be selected using OUTDEVICE
params: Print the CRC-16 (Cyclic Redundancy Check) of the file.
Get the amount of free space on the SD card

Finds the first entry in the directory structure of the

Finds the next entry in the directory structure of the
  specified file type

Finds the previous entry in the directory structure of
  the specified file type
Format the SD card using the FAT32 structure
Loads the specified program to the controllers memory
Loads the specified project into the controllers memory

Return the size of a file on the SD Card
