name: I_GAIN
description: Used as part of the closed loop control, adding integral gain to a system reduces position error when at rest or moving steadily. It will produce or increase overshoot and may lead to oscillation. For an integral gain Ki and a sum of position errors ∫ e, the contribution to the output signal is: O i = K i × ∫ e
examples:
P_GAIN=1
I_GAIN=0.01
D_GAIN=0
OV_GAIN=0
…

name: JERK
description: The JERK axis parameter is the derivative of acceleration. JERK units are /sec/sec/sec and specifies the rate at which acceleration and deceleration should change. JERK is applied only when using VP_MODE 5 or 6, with 0 using SRAMP to smooth motion. As of firmware version 2.0342, the SRAMP keyword may be used instead of JERK, but if SRAMP is assigned a value (even 0), then any assignment to JERK will be ignored. When SRAMP is used, the JERK is calculated from ACCEL / SRAMP. Using SRAMP with VP_MODE = 6 is intended solely to aid portability for existing applications, and it is strongly recommended that new applications use JERK.
examples:
VP_MODE = 6


UNITS = 1000


SPEED = 50000


ACCEL = 80000


DECEL = 80000


JERK = 300000





MOVE(50000)

name: JERK_FACTOR
description: This parameter is used to reduce the jerk specified in JERK axis parameter by the per-unit value entered when VP_MODE is 5 or 6. Jerk factor range is 0-1, where 1 represents using 100% of the jerk value.
examples:
IF VR(200) = 1 THEN
'Start the jerk control routine
BASE(2)
REPEAT
JERK_FACTOR = AIN(0) / 4096
WA(100)
UNTIL VR(200) = 0
ENDIF
JERK_FACTOR AXIS(4) = 0.5

name: JOGSPEED
description: Sets the jog speed in user units for an axis to run at when performing a jog. You can set a faster jog speed using SPEED and the FAST_JOG input
examples:
BASE(12)
SPEED=3000
FWD_JOG = 12
JOGSPEED = 20

name: KEY
description: Key is used to check if there are characters in a channel buffer. This command does not read the character but allows the program to test if any character has arrived. A TRUE result will be reset when the character is read with GET .
params: See # for the full channel list (default 0 if omitted)

If channel is a FIFO opened in character mode, then value
  is a negative value representing the number of characters in the channel
  buffer.


  

If channel is a FIFO opened in line mode, then value is a
  negative value representing the number of lines in the channel buffer.


  

Only for channels #40 to #48 (file channels) when working
  as a FIFO. See 
OPEN
 for a description of the FIFO
  modes.

-1 (
TRUE
) if there are characters
  in the buffer except for the above specific case.

0 (
FALSE
) if there are no
  characters.
examples:
main:
IF KEY #1 THEN GOSUB read
...
read:
GET #1, k
RETURN

name: LAST_AXIS
description: The Motion coordinator keeps a list of axes that are currently in use. LAST_AXIS can be used to read the number of the highest axis in the list or assigned to set the last axis. LAST_AXIS is set automatically by the system software when an axis is written to; this can include setting BASE for the axis. Axes higher than LAST_AXIS are not processed. Not all axis lower than LAST_AXIS are processed.
examples:
IF LAST_AXIS <> 26 THEN
PRINT#user, "Digital Drives not initialised"
ENDIF
LAST_AXIS = 0

IF LAST_AXIS <> 26 THEN
PRINT#user, "Digital Drives not initialised"
ENDIF
LAST_AXIS = 0

name: LCASE
description: Returns a new string with the input string converted to all lower case.
examples:
DIM str1 AS STRING(32)
str1 = "TRIO MOTION TECHNOLOGY"
PRINT LCASE(str1)

DIM str1 AS STRING(32)
str1 = "TRIO MOTION TECHNOLOGY"
PRINT LCASE(str1)

name: LCDSTR
description: Allows the currently displayed character string on display to be read from or written to when under user control. This will only be allowed when the display is in normal display mode, for example if the user removes and replaces the ethernet cable then the displaying of IP address data will take priority before returning to the previous display string again. Note, this function is available on the MC405 only.
examples:
DISPLAY.16 = 1 'Enable user control of 7-segment chars
vr(100) = -88
LCDSTR = STR(VR(100), 0, 3)

DISPLAY.16 = 1 'Enable user control of 7-segment chars
vr(100) = -88
LCDSTR = STR(VR(100), 0, 3)

name: LEFT
description: Returns the left most section of the specified string using the length specified.
params: Length of string to be returned
examples:
DIM str1 AS STRING(32)
str1 = "TRIO MOTION TECHNOLOGY"
PRINT LEFT(str1, 4)

DIM str1 AS STRING(32)
str1 = "TRIO MOTION TECHNOLOGY"
PRINT LEFT(str1, 4)

name: LEN
description: Returns number of characters in the specified string. Note that this will differ from the number of bytes used to store the string when UTF-8 characters are present.
examples:
DIM str1 AS STRING(20)
str1 = "MyString"
x = LEN(str1) 'x will be 8

DIM str1 AS STRING(20)
str1 = "MyString"
x = LEN(str1) 'x will be 8

name: LENB
description: Returns number of bytes used in the specified string. Note that this will differ from the number of characters in the string when UTF-8 characters are present.
examples:
DIM str1 AS STRING(30)
str1 = "Здравей
свят" '"Hello world" in Bulgarian
PRINT LENB(str1), LEN(str1) 'Will display 23 and 12

DIM str1 AS STRING(30)
str1 = "Здравей
свят" '"Hello world" in Bulgarian
PRINT LENB(str1), LEN(str1) 'Will display 23 and 12

name: LIMIT_BUFFERED
description: This sets the maximum number of moves that the controller can buffer. You can increase the machine speed when using MERGE or CORNER_MODE by increasing the number of moves buffered. Each controller has its own maximum buffer size, indicated by BUFFERSIZE .
examples: LIMIT_BUFFERED = 10

name: LINK_AXIS
description: Returns the axis number that the axis is linked to during any linked moves. Linked moves are where the demand position is a function of another axis e.g. CONNECT , CAMBOX , MOVELINK

name: LINPUT
description: Waits for an input string and stores the ASCII values of the string in an array of variables starting at a specified numbered variable. The string must be terminated with a carriage return <CR> which is also stored. The string is not echoed by the controller. You can print the string from the VR s using VRSTRING
params: See # for the full channel list (default 0 if omitted)

The 
VR
 variable to store the
  received character
examples:
LINPUT #5, VR(0)
VR(0)    83    ASCII 'S'
VR(1)    84    ASCII 'T'
VR(2)    65    ASCII 'A'
VR(3)    82    ASCII 'R'
VR(4)    84    ASCII 'T'
VR(5)    13    ASCII carriage return

name: LIST
description: Prints the current SELECT ed program or a specified program to the current output channel. Usually you will view a program by using Motion Perfect.
params: The name of the program to print

name: LIST_GLOBAL
description: Prints the selected GLOBAL and CONSTANT definitions to the current output channel.
params: Prints all the defined GLOBAL and CONSTANT values.

Number of the first GLOBAL or CONSTANT value to be
  printed.

Number of GLOBAL and CONSTANT values to be printed. If
  length is -1 then all values are printed.
examples:
CONSTANT "cutter", 23
GLOBAL "conveyor",5
>>LIST_GLOBAL
Global                           VR
----------------                 ----
conveyor                         5
Constant                         Value
----------------                 -------
cutter                           23.00000
>>LIST_GLOBAL 1
Global                           VR
----------------                 ----
conveyor                         5
>>LIST_GLOBAL 0,1
Constant                         Value
----------------                 -------
cutter                           23.00000
>>

name: LN
description: Returns the natural logarithm of the expression.
examples: VR(0) = LN(a*b)

name: LOADED
description: Checks if all the movements have been loaded into the MTYPE buffer so will return a TRUE value when there are no buffered movements, otherwise FALSE . Although it is possible to use LOADED as part of any expression it is typically used with a WAIT .
examples:
WHILE machine_on
WAIT UNTIL LOADED OR NOT machine_off
IF machine_on THEN
MOVE(TABLE(position)
position = position + 1
ENDIF
WEND

name: LOADSYSTEM
description: Used by Motion Perfect to load Firmware to the controller If you wish to load firmware without Motion Perfect you can use the SD card (FILE command)

name: LOAD_PROJECT
description: Used by Motion Perfect to load projects to the controller. If you wish to load projects outside of Motion Perfect use the Autoloader ActiveX

name: LOCK
description: The LOCK copmmand is designed to prevent programs from being viewed or modified by personnel unaware of the security code. The lock code number is stored in the flash EPROM. When a Motion Coordinator is locked, it is not possible to view, edit or save any programs and command line instructions are limited to those required to execute the program. The CONTROL value has 1000 added to it when the controller is LOCKed. You should use Motion Perfect to LOCK and UNLOCK your controller. To unlock the Motion Coordinator, the UNLOCK command should be entered using the same lock code number which was used originally to LOCK it. The lock code number may be any integer and is held in encoded form. Once LOCKed, the only way to gain full access to the Motion Coordinator is to UNLOCK it with the correct code. For best security the lock number should be 7 digits. It is possible to compromise the security of the lock system. Users must consider if the level of security is sufficient to protect their programs. If you want better security consider encrypting your project. If you forget the security code number, the Motion Coordinator may have to be returned to your supplier to be unlocked.

name: LOOKAHEAD_FACTOR
description: This parameter is used to modify all speeds in the motion buffer by factoring them by value specified. LOOKAHEAD_FACTOR operates with all SP type interpolated moves MOVESP , MOVEABSSP and MHELICALSP and will factor the axis speeds FORCE_SPEED , ENDMOVE_SPEED and STARTMOVE_SPEED . This is typically used to reduce speeds by using a factor less than one but can also be used to increase speeds by using a factor greater than one.
examples:
IF VR(200) = 1 THEN
'Start the operator speed control routine
BASE(2)
REPEAT
speed_factor = AIN(0) / 4096
LOOKAHEAD_FACTOR = speed_factor
WA(100)
UNTIL VR(200) = 0
LOOKAHEAD_FACTOR = 1.0 '100% full speed
ENDIF
LOOKAHEAD_FACTOR AXIS(4) = 0.5

name: LOOKUP
description: The LOOKUP command is used by Motion Perfect to access the local variables on an executing process. You should use the variable watch window in Motion Perfect to access the variables on an executing process.
params:
Prints (in binary) floating point value from an
  expression
Prints (in binary) integer value from an expression
Prints (in binary) local variable from a process
Returns to BASIC local variable from a process

Either an expression string (format=0 or 1) or the
  offset number of the local variable into the processes local variable list.

name: <  Less Than
description: Returns TRUE if <expression-1> is less than <expression-2>, otherwise returns FALSE . The comparison operators are typically used in conditional statements like IF , REPEAT and WHILE , though can be used anywhere an expression is valid.
examples: IF AIN(1) < 10 THEN GOSUB rollup

name: <=  Less Than or Equal
description: Returns TRUE if <expression-1> is less than or equal to <expression-2>, otherwise returns FALSE . The comparison operators are typically used in conditional statements like IF , REPEAT and WHILE , though can be used anywhere an expression is valid.

name: MARK
description: This parameter can be polled to determine if the registration event has occurred. MARK is reset when REGIST is executed
examples:
loop:
WAIT UNTIL IN(punch_clr)=ON
MOVE(index_length)
REGIST(20, 0, 0, 0, 0) 'rising edge of R
WAIT UNTIL MARK
MOVEMODIFY(REG_POS + offset)
WAIT IDLE
GOTO loop
' REGIST(20, channel, source, edge, window [,quantity,
table_start])
' capture 60 events and store positions starting at
TABLE(400)
REGIST(20, 32, 4, 0, 0, 60, 400)
REPEAT
VR(30) = -MARK
VR(31) = 400 + VR(30) - 1 ' the current position in
TABLE
UNTIL VR(30) > 59

name: MARKB
description: This parameter can be polled to determine if the registration event has occurred on the second registration channel. MARKB is reset when REGIST is executed.

name: MERGE
description: Velocity profiled moves can be MERGEd together so that the speed will not ramp down to zero between the current move and the buffered move. It is up to the programmer to ensure that the merging is sensible. For example merging a MOVE(distance) with a MOVE(-distance) will cause an attempted instantaneous change of direction. MERGE will only function if: The next move is loaded into the buffer The Axis Group does not change on multi-axis moves If a 2 axis move is followed by a 3 axis move, or similar change in the number of axes, this results in a change to the Axis Group. Velocity profiled moves ( MOVE , MOVEABS , MOVECIRC , MHELICAL , REVERSE , FORWARD ) cannot be merged with linked moves ( CONNECT , MOVELINK , CAMBOX ) When merging multi-axis moves only the base axis MERGE flag needs to be set. If you are merging short moves you may need to increase the number of buffered moves by increasing LIMIT_BUFFERED
examples:
BASE(0,1) 'set base array
MERGE=ON 'set MERGE state
MOVEABS(0,50) 'run a sequence of moves
MOVE(0,100)
MOVECIRC(50,50,50,0,1)
MOVE(100,0)
MOVECIRC(50,-50,0,-50,1)
MOVE(0,-100)
MOVECIRC(-50,-50,-50,0,1)
MOVE(-100,0)
MOVECIRC(-50,50,0,50,1)
WAIT IDLE
MERGE=OFF
BASE(0, 1, 2) 'set base array
MERGE=ON 'set MERGE state
MOVEABS(0,50) ' When this move ends, the axis will decel
to 0
MOVE(0, 120, 20)
MHELICAL(50, 50, 50, 0, 1, 0) ' Arc on XY with 0 on Z

name: MHELICAL
syntax: MHELICAL(end1, end2, centre1, centre2, direction, distance3 [,mode])
description: Performs a helical move. Moves 2 orthogonal axes in such a way as to produce a circular arc at the tool point with a simultaneous linear move on a third axis. The first 5 parameters are like those of an MOVECIRC command. The sixth parameter defines the simultaneous linear movement normal to the plane of the circle (the helical component). Multi-axis interpolation uses the UNITS, SPEED, ACCEL and DECEL of the BASE axis. For correct XYZ vector operation, the UNITS of all 3 axes must be the same.
params:
position on 
BASE
 axis to finish
  at.

position on next axis in 
BASE

  array to finish at.

position on 
BASE
 axis about
  which to move.

position on next axis in 
BASE

  array about which to move.
Arc is interpolated in an anti-clockwise direction
Arc is interpolated in a clockwise direction

The distance to move on the third axis in the 
BASE
 array axis in user units

Interpolate the 3rd axis with the main 2 axes when
  calculating path speed. (True helical path)

Interpolate only the first 2 axes for path speed, but
  move the 3rd axis in coordination with the other 2 axes. (Circular path with
  following 3rd axis)
examples:
REP_DIST AXIS(3) = 360
REP_OPTION AXIS(3) = ON 'All 3 axes must be homed before
starting
MERGE = ON
MOVEABS(360) AXIS(3) 'Point axis 3 in correct starting
direction
WAIT IDLE AXIS(3)
MOVE(0, 12)
MHELICAL(3, 3, 3, 0, 1, 90)
MOVE(16, 0)
MHELICAL(3, -3, 0, -3, 1, 90)
MOVE(0, -6)
MHELICAL(-3, -3, -3, 0, 1, 90)
MOVE(-2, 0)
MHELICAL(-3, 3, 0, 3, 1, 90)
BASE(0, 1, 2)
MERGE = ON           'Merge moves into one continuous
movement
MOVE(50, 0)
MHELICAL(0, -6, 0, -3, 1, 180, 1)
MOVE(-22, 0)
WAIT IDLE
MOVE(-90) AXIS(2)    'Rotate the knife after stopping at
corner
WAIT IDLE AXIS(2)
MOVE(0, -50)
MHELICAL(-6, 0, -3, 0, 1, 180, 1)
MOVE(0, 50)
WAIT IDLE            'Pause again to rotate the knife
MOVE(-90) AXIS(2)
WAIT IDLE AXIS(2)
MOVE(-22, 0)
MHELICAL(0, 6, 0, 3, 1, 180, 1)
WAIT IDLE

REP_DIST AXIS(3) = 360
REP_OPTION AXIS(3) = ON 'All 3 axes must be homed before
starting
MERGE = ON
MOVEABS(360) AXIS(3) 'Point axis 3 in correct starting
direction
WAIT IDLE AXIS(3)
MOVE(0, 12)
MHELICAL(3, 3, 3, 0, 1, 90)
MOVE(16, 0)
MHELICAL(3, -3, 0, -3, 1, 90)
MOVE(0, -6)
MHELICAL(-3, -3, -3, 0, 1, 90)
MOVE(-2, 0)
MHELICAL(-3, 3, 0, 3, 1, 90)
BASE(0, 1, 2)
MERGE = ON           'Merge moves into one continuous
movement
MOVE(50, 0)
MHELICAL(0, -6, 0, -3, 1, 180, 1)
MOVE(-22, 0)
WAIT IDLE
MOVE(-90) AXIS(2)    'Rotate the knife after stopping at
corner
WAIT IDLE AXIS(2)
MOVE(0, -50)
MHELICAL(-6, 0, -3, 0, 1, 180, 1)
MOVE(0, 50)
WAIT IDLE            'Pause again to rotate the knife
MOVE(-90) AXIS(2)
WAIT IDLE AXIS(2)
MOVE(-22, 0)
MHELICAL(0, 6, 0, 3, 1, 180, 1)
WAIT IDLE

name: MHELICALSP
syntax: MHELICALSP(end1, end2, centre1, centre2, direction, distance3[, mode])
description: Performs a helical move as per MHELICAL but with advanced speed control (allows vector speed to be changed when using multiple moves in the buffer), using the axis parameters FORCE_SPEED , ENDMOVE_SPEED . and STARTMOVE_SPEED . Multi-axis interpolation uses the UNITS, SPEED, ACCEL and DECEL of the BASE axis. For correct XYZ vector operation, the UNITS of all 3 axes must be the same.
examples:
FORCE_SPEED = 40
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 20
MHELICALSP(100, 100, 0, 100, 1, 100)

name: MICROSTEP
description: Discontinued.

name: MID
description: Returns the mid-section of the specified string using the optional length specified, or defaults to the remainder of the string when not specified.
params:
Length of string to be returned, if not specified then
  the remainder of the string will be used
examples:
DIM str1 AS STRING(32)
str1 = "TRIO MOTION TECHNOLOGY"
PRINT MID(str1, 5, 6)
DIM str1 AS STRING(32)
DIM vr_start AS INTEGER
DIM string_length AS INTEGER
DIM i AS INTEGER
str1 = "My cat is black"
vr_start = 10
string_length = LEN(str1)
FOR i = 0 TO string_length - 1
VR(vr_start + i) = MID(str1, i, 1)
NEXT i
VR(vr_start + string_length) = 0 'terminator byte
PRINT VRSTRING(vr_start)

DIM str1 AS STRING(32)
str1 = "TRIO MOTION TECHNOLOGY"
PRINT MID(str1, 5, 6)

DIM str1 AS STRING(32)
DIM vr_start AS INTEGER
DIM string_length AS INTEGER
DIM i AS INTEGER
str1 = "My cat is black"
vr_start = 10
string_length = LEN(str1)
FOR i = 0 TO string_length - 1
VR(vr_start + i) = MID(str1, i, 1)
NEXT i
VR(vr_start + string_length) = 0 'terminator byte
PRINT VRSTRING(vr_start)

name: MOD
description: Returns the integer modulus of an expression, this is the value after the integer has wrapped around the modulus
params:
Any valid TrioBASIC expression used as the value to apply
  the modulus to.
Any valid TrioBASIC expression used as the modulus

name: MODBUS
description: This function allows the user to configure the Ethernet port to run as a Modbus TCP and Modbus RTU Client (Master). Using the MODBUS command, the user can open a connection to a remote server, transfer data using a sub-set of Modbus Function Numbers and check for errors. Up to 10 Modbus TCP connections may be opened simultaneously. One Modbus RTU master connection may be opened. When attempting to open a Modbus connection over the serial port, you must first configure the serial port parameters using the SETCOM command.

name: MODBUS_TX_DELAY
description: This parameter operates on the Modbus RTU (serial) server. It is the delay in milliseconds between the incoming Modbus request packet and the outgoing response. There is a built-in delay of approximately 3 ms. The value entered in this parameter will be added to the normal delay. Set this delay if the HMI needs some time to switch to receive mode. Especially useful to tune the timings on RS485 2-wire Modbus.
params:
Time in milliseconds between end of request and start
  of response.
examples:
MODBUS_TX_DELAY = 7 'There is a built-in 3 ms delay
ADDRESS = 1
SETCOM(19200, 8, 1, 0, 2, 4, 0, 3, 1)

MODBUS_TX_DELAY = 7 'There is a built-in 3 ms delay
ADDRESS = 1
SETCOM(19200, 8, 1, 0, 2, 4, 0, 3, 1)

name: MODULEIO_BASE
description: This parameter sets the start address of any expansion module I/O channels. Together with CANIO_BASE , DRIVEIO_BASE and NODE_IO the I/O allocation scheme can replace and expand the behaviour of MODULE_IO_MODE , however MODULE_IO_MODE takes precedence if its value has been changed to 2 (CANIO followed by MODULEIO).

name: MODULE_IO_MODE
description: This parameter sets the start address of any expansion module I/O channels. You can also turn off module I/O for backwards compatibility. Note that extended I/O mapping functionality is available using MC_CONFIG parameters CANIO_BASE , DRIVEIO_BASE , MODULEIO_BASE and NODE_IO . These replace the need to use MODULE_IO_MODE and provide control over exactly where I/O points are positioned within the Controller I/O map. However, if MODULE_IO_MODE is set to 2 then this takes precedence over the positioning of CANIO and MODULEIO via CANIO_BASE and MODULEIO_BASE . This parameter is stored in Flash EPROM and can be included in the MC_CONFIG script.

name: MOTION_ERROR
description: The MOTION_ERROR system parameter is a 64-bit integer that provides an indication that one or more axes are in error, with a bit being set for each axis in error. Since this parameter is a bit mask, it is usual to use the bit operator to interrogate each bit. In order to handle up to 1024 axes, MOTION_ERROR is split into 16 banks of 64-bit integers with each bank containing the motion error state of 64 axes. To access a bank, MOTION_ERROR can be indexed using an index from 0 to 15. As with the unindexed form, the bit operator may be used in conjunction with indexing.
params: The bank of axes to access, range 0 to 15 (default 0).

name: MOTION_ERROR_COUNT
description: This system parameter returns an integer indicating the number of motion errors present.

name: MOVE
description: Incremental move. One axis or multiple axes move at the programmed speed and acceleration for a distance specified as an increment from the end of the last specified move. The first parameter in the list is sent to the BASE axis, the second to the next axis in the BASE array, and so on. In the multi-axis form, the speed and acceleration employed for the movement are taken from the first axis in the BASE group. The speeds of each axis are controlled so as to make the resulting vector of the movement run at the SPEED setting. Multi-axis interpolation uses the UNITS, SPEED, ACCEL and DECEL of the BASE axis. For correct XYZ vector operation, the UNITS of all 3 axes must be the same. If an axis needs to be excluded in the vector speed calculation, then INTERP_FACTOR can be set to 0 for that axis. Uninterpolated, unsynchronised multi-axis motion can be achieved by simply placing MOVE commands on each axis independently. If needed, the target axis for an individual MOVE can be specified using the AXIS command modifier. This overrides the BASE axis setting for one MOVE only. The distance values specified are scaled using the unit conversion factor axis parameter; UNITS . Therefore if, for example, an axis has 400 encoder edges/mm and UNITS for that axis are 400, the command MOVE(12.5) would move 12.5 mm. When MERGE is set to ON , individual moves in the same axis group are merged together to make a continuous path movement. If the number of axes in the move that follows on from the previous move are different, then they will not MERGE. Likewise, a FORWARD or REVERSE will not MERGE with a MOVE or MOVEABS.
params: distance to move on base axis from current position.

distance to move on next axis in BASE array from
  current position.

distance to move on next axis in BASE array from
  current position.

distance to move on next axis in BASE array from
  current position.

name: MOVEABS
description: Absolute position move. Move one axis or multiple axes to position(s) referenced with respect to the zero (home) position. The first parameter in the list is sent to the axis specified with the AXIS command or to the current BASE axis, the second to the next axis, and so on. In the multi-axis form, the speed, acceleration, and deceleration employed for the movement are taken from the first axis in the BASE group. The speeds of each axis are controlled to make the resulting vector of the movement run at the SPEED setting. Multi-axis interpolation uses the UNITS, SPEED, ACCEL and DECEL of the BASE axis. For correct XYZ vector operation, the UNITS of all 3 axes must be the same. If an axis needs to be not included in the vector speed calculation, then INTERP_FACTOR can be set to 0 for that axis. Uninterpolated, unsynchronised multi-axis motion can be achieved by simply placing MOVEABS commands on each axis independently. If needed, the target axis for an individual MOVEABS can be specified using the AXIS command. This overrides the BASE axis setting for one MOVEABS only. The values specified are scaled using the unit conversion factor axis parameter; UNITS . Therefore if, for example, an axis has 400 encoder edges/mm the UNITS for that axis is 400. The command MOVEABS(6) would then move to a position 6 mm from the zero position. When MERGE is set to ON , absolute and relative moves are merged to make a continuous path movement. The position of the axes’ zero(home) positions can be changed by the commands: OFFPOS , DEFPOS , REP_DIST , REP_OPTION , and DATUM . If the number of axes in the move that follows on from the previous move are different, then they will not MERGE. Likewise, a FORWARD, REVERSE or any linked move (e.g., MOVELINK) will not MERGE with a MOVE or MOVEABS. The MOVEABS will go to the wrong endpoint.
params: position to move to on base axis.

position to move to on next axis in 
BASE

  array.

position to move to on next axis in 
BASE

  array.

position to move to on next axis in 
BASE

  array
examples:
'Define absolute positions
Home = 1000
position_1 = 2000
position_2 = 3000
WHILE IN(run_switch) = ON
IF IN(6) = ON THEN       'Switch 6 selects position 2
MOVEABS(position_2)
WAIT IDLE
ELSEIF IN(7) = ON THEN   'Switch 7 selects position 1
MOVEABS(position_1)
WAIT IDLE
ELSE
MOVEABS(home)
WAIT IDLE
ENDIF
WEND
MOVEABS(28.5, 350) 'Move to just outside the pen holder
area
WAIT IDLE
SPEED = pen_pickup_speed
MOVEABS(20.5, 350) 'Move in to pick up the pen
FOR x = 0 TO 5
FOR y = 0 TO 7
MOVEABS(-340, -516.5)     'Move to pick-up point
WAIT IDLE
GOSUB pick                'Call pick up subroutine
PRINT "Move to Position: "; x * 6 + y + 1
MOVEABS(x * 185, y * 185) 'Move to position in grid
WAIT IDLE
GOSUB place               'Call place down subroutine
NEXT y
NEXT x

'Define absolute positions
Home = 1000
position_1 = 2000
position_2 = 3000
WHILE IN(run_switch) = ON
IF IN(6) = ON THEN       'Switch 6 selects position 2
MOVEABS(position_2)
WAIT IDLE
ELSEIF IN(7) = ON THEN   'Switch 7 selects position 1
MOVEABS(position_1)
WAIT IDLE
ELSE
MOVEABS(home)
WAIT IDLE
ENDIF
WEND

MOVEABS(28.5, 350) 'Move to just outside the pen holder
area
WAIT IDLE
SPEED = pen_pickup_speed
MOVEABS(20.5, 350) 'Move in to pick up the pen

FOR x = 0 TO 5
FOR y = 0 TO 7
MOVEABS(-340, -516.5)     'Move to pick-up point
WAIT IDLE
GOSUB pick                'Call pick up subroutine
PRINT "Move to Position: "; x * 6 + y + 1
MOVEABS(x * 185, y * 185) 'Move to position in grid
WAIT IDLE
GOSUB place               'Call place down subroutine
NEXT y
NEXT x

name: MOVEABSFR
description: MOVEABS in a FRAME. Under development, use only on instruction from Trio.

name: MOVEABSSEQ
syntax:
MOVEABSSEQ(table_index, axes, npoints, options, radius[, output[,
transition_angle]])
description: The MOVEABSSEQ command allows a sequence of movements to be loaded via TABLE values. The moves can be automatically merged together using a circular or spherical arc. Movement on 2 to 6 axes may be controlled in this way. The MOVEABSSEQ is loaded into the controller move buffers as a sequence of MOVEABS->MOVECIRC-> moves if 2 axes are specified and MOVEABS->MSPHERICAL-> if 3 or more axes are specified. The linear move may be omitted if the arcs blend together. If “Options” bit 0 is set to 1, the move sequence loaded will be a sequence of MOVEABSSP->MOVECIRCSP-> moves if 2 axes are specified and MOVEABSSP->MSPHERICALSP-> if 3+ axes are specified. If Options bit 0 is set, 4 additional speed control values must be provided with each table point. MOVE_COUNT is incremented on every move loaded. This parameter may be set by a user program.
params:
Index in TABLE memory where the absolute positions
  start.

The number of points, each point requires 2 or 3 table
  values.

0 = Sequence will load MOVEABS, MOVECIRC, etc.


  

1 = Sequence will load embedded speed moves MOVEABSSP,
  MOVECIRCSP, etc.

0 = Load a sequence of standard move types.


  

1 = Load a sequence of MOVEABSSEQ/MOVEABSSEQSP contour

0 = No MOVE_PA/MOVE_PB data in TABLE


  

1 = MOVE_PA/MOVE_PB data is embedded within the TABLE
  data

0 = Process the move and load it


  

1 = Process the move but without loading it


  

Only applicable when bit 2 is set

0 = No embedded speeds


  

1 = Embed speeds from TABLE data


  

Only applicable when bit 2 is set

0 = No embedded MOVETURN


  

1 = Embed MOVETURN (default is mode 5 unless bit 16 is
  set)

0 = Axes above XYZ are linear


  

1 = Axes above XYZ are in degrees

Contains a 7-bit integer that specifies an offset
  between data points other than "Axes"

0 = Use radius parameter


  

1 = Radius is embedded within the TABLE data

0 = Use MOVETURN mode 5 (default)


  

1 = Use MOVETURN mode 6

The radius of the corner fillet (see option bit 14,
  which can override the value supplied in this parameter)

Optional output of data to TABLE.  0 means do not
  output data to TABLE.  Value otherwise is the TABLE start index for the data.

Optional specification of transition angle (in Radians)
  for any filleted arcs that are generated.  Either do not supply a parameter
  or set it to 0 if no transition curve is to be applied.
examples:
'Using MOVE and MOVECIRC
MOVE(0, 60) 'move A -> B
MOVECIRC(30, 30, 30, 0, 1) 'move B -> C
MOVE(20, 0) ' move C -> D
MOVECIRC(30, -30, 0, -30, 1) 'move D -> E
MOVE(0, -60) 'move E -> F
MOVECIRC(-30, -30, -30, 0, 1) 'move F -> G
MOVE(-20, 0) 'move G -> H
MOVECIRC(-30, 30, 0, 30, 1) 'move H -> A
WAIT IDLE
DEFPOS(100, 30)
WAIT UNTIL OFFPOS = 0
'Equivalent using MOVEABSSEQ
TABLE(1000, 100, 120)
TABLE(1002, 180, 120)
TABLE(1004, 180, 0)
TABLE(1006, 100, 0)
TABLE(1008, 100, 30)
MOVEABSSEQ(1000, 2, 5, 0, 30)
UNITS AXIS(0) = 1000
UNITS AXIS(1) = 1000
BASE(0, 1)
MERGE = ON
trans = 0
SPEED = 2500
ACCEL = 100000
DECEL = 100000
lin_sp = SPEED
lin_end = SPEED * 0.5
arc_sp = SPEED * 0.5
arc_end = SPEED * 0.5
TABLE(1020, 0, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1026, 180, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1032, 360, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1038, 360, 360, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1044, 0, 360, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1050, 0, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1056, -180, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
MOVEABSSEQ(1020, 2, 7, 1, 80, 0, trans * PI / 180)
'Suppose an ellipse is defined as a sequence of points 1
degree apart:
UNITS AXIS(0) = 1000
UNITS AXIS(1) = 1000
SPEED = 500
ACCEL = 50000
DECEL = 50000
SRAMP=30
DEFPOS(100,0)
a = 100
b = 50
FOR d = 0 TO 360
x = COS(d * PI / 180) * a
y = SIN(d * PI / 180) * b
TABLE(1000 + d * 2, x, y)
NEXT d
MOVEABSSEQ(1000, 2, 361, 4, 0, 2000)
FOR a = 0 TO 5
ATYPE AXIS(a) = 0
UNITS AXIS(a) = 1000
NEXT a
'Ignore uvw for speed calculations:
INTERP_FACTOR AXIS(3) = 0
INTERP_FACTOR AXIS(4) = 0
INTERP_FACTOR AXIS(5) = 0
'This program has data sets 10 values apart :
TABLE(100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 15, 0)
TABLE(110, 10, 0, 0, 10, -5, 0)
TABLE(120, 10, 10, 0, 0, 0, 10)
TABLE(130, 20, 10, 0, 20, 5, 10)
TABLE(140, 20, 0, 0, 0, 0, 10)
TABLE(150, 20, 0, 15, 5, 5, 0)
BASE(0)
'Define start position:
DEFPOS(0, 10, 0, 0, 20, 0, 0)
MOVE_COUNT = 0
SPEED = 5
ACC(SPEED * 50)
MERGE = ON
options = 10 * 256 'Set to use data 10 values apart
movet = 0
npoints = 6
axes = 6
radius = 2
MOVEABSSEQ(100, axes, npoints, options, radius)
MOVEABS(20, 0, 30, 5, 5, 0) 'This MOVEABS will be MERGE'd
together with the MOVEABSSEQ

'Using MOVE and MOVECIRC
MOVE(0, 60) 'move A -> B
MOVECIRC(30, 30, 30, 0, 1) 'move B -> C
MOVE(20, 0) ' move C -> D
MOVECIRC(30, -30, 0, -30, 1) 'move D -> E
MOVE(0, -60) 'move E -> F
MOVECIRC(-30, -30, -30, 0, 1) 'move F -> G
MOVE(-20, 0) 'move G -> H
MOVECIRC(-30, 30, 0, 30, 1) 'move H -> A
WAIT IDLE
DEFPOS(100, 30)
WAIT UNTIL OFFPOS = 0
'Equivalent using MOVEABSSEQ
TABLE(1000, 100, 120)
TABLE(1002, 180, 120)
TABLE(1004, 180, 0)
TABLE(1006, 100, 0)
TABLE(1008, 100, 30)
MOVEABSSEQ(1000, 2, 5, 0, 30)

UNITS AXIS(0) = 1000
UNITS AXIS(1) = 1000
BASE(0, 1)
MERGE = ON
trans = 0
SPEED = 2500
ACCEL = 100000
DECEL = 100000
lin_sp = SPEED
lin_end = SPEED * 0.5
arc_sp = SPEED * 0.5
arc_end = SPEED * 0.5
TABLE(1020, 0, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1026, 180, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1032, 360, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1038, 360, 360, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1044, 0, 360, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1050, 0, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
TABLE(1056, -180, 0, lin_sp, lin_end, arc_sp, arc_end)
MOVEABSSEQ(1020, 2, 7, 1, 80, 0, trans * PI / 180)

'Suppose an ellipse is defined as a sequence of points 1
degree apart:
UNITS AXIS(0) = 1000
UNITS AXIS(1) = 1000
SPEED = 500
ACCEL = 50000
DECEL = 50000
SRAMP=30
DEFPOS(100,0)
a = 100
b = 50
FOR d = 0 TO 360
x = COS(d * PI / 180) * a
y = SIN(d * PI / 180) * b
TABLE(1000 + d * 2, x, y)

ATYPE AXIS(a) = 0
UNITS AXIS(a) = 1000
NEXT a
'Ignore uvw for speed calculations:
INTERP_FACTOR AXIS(3) = 0
INTERP_FACTOR AXIS(4) = 0
INTERP_FACTOR AXIS(5) = 0
'This program has data sets 10 values apart :
TABLE(100, 0, 0, 0, 0, 15, 0)

together with the MOVEABSSEQ

name: MOVEABSSP
description: Works as MOVEABS and additionally allows vector speed to be changed when using multiple moves in the look ahead buffer when MERGE = ON , using additional parameters FORCE_SPEED , ENDMOVE_SPEED and STARTMOVE_SPEED . Multi-axis interpolation uses the UNITS, SPEED, ACCEL and DECEL of the BASE axis. For correct XYZ vector operation, the UNITS of all 3 axes must be the same. If an axis needs to be not included in the vector speed calculation, then INTERP_FACTOR can be set to 0 for that axis. The values specified are scaled using the unit conversion factor axis parameter; UNITS . Therefore if, for example, an axis has 400 encoder edges/mm the UNITS for that axis is 400. The command MOVEABS(6) would then move to a position 6 mm from the zero position. When MERGE is set to ON , absolute and relative moves are merged together to make a continuous path movement. Absolute moves are converted to incremental moves as they enter the buffer. This is essential as the vector length is required to calculate the start of deceleration. It should be noted that if any move in the buffer is cancelled by the programmer, the absolute position will not be achieved. If the number of axes in the move that follows on from the previous move are different, then they will not MERGE. Likewise, a FORWARD, REVERSE or any linked move (e.g. MOVELINK) will not MERGE with a MOVESP or MOVEABSSP. The MOVEABSSP will go to the wrong endpoint.
params: position to move to on base axis.

position to move to on next axis in 
BASE

  array.

position to move to on next axis in 
BASE

  array.

position to move to on next axis in 
BASE

  array
examples:
FORCE_SPEED = 40
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 20
MOVEABSSP(100, 100)

name: MOVEABSSPCONT
description: Under development, use only on instruction from Trio.
params: Position to move to on base axis.

name: MOVEADD
description: MOVEADD inserts an additional change of position on top of a move that is already running on the axis. The distance moved is in the same user units as the main move, i.e. if the axis is set up by UNITS to be in mm, then MOVEADD adds distance in mm, speeds in mm/s and accelerations in mm/s 2. MOVEADD uses the demands of the axis it is being added to, but these can be overridden by using FORCE_SPEED, FORCE_ACCEL, FORCE_DECEL and FORCE_JERK. Note that MOVEADD is not an advanced speed control move, and uses the FORCE_SPEED, FORCE_ACCEL, FORCE_DECEL and FORCE_JERK purely as a convenient means of supplying MOVEADD its own demands in the case that they need to differ from the demands of axis having the additional movement. Note that MOVEADD does not adhere to the VP_MODE setting of the axis it is being added to, instead using its own S-ramped VPU. The action of MOVEADD is like loading a MOVE on a virtual axis and connecting it to the main axis using ADDAX, but without the need of a virtual axis. Prefer using a virtual axis and ADDAX to MOVEADD, as this gives extra flexibility with regard how the move is handled (choice of demands, VP_MODE, cancellation, etc.). To cancel both the move and the additional movement, use CANCEL (5). To cancel the additional movement part way through its programmed distance, use CANCEL (6).
params:
Relative distance of the added movement. A positive
  value will advance the axis, and a negative value will retard the axis.
examples:
FORWARD
WA(100)
FORCE_SPEED = 50
FORCE_ACCEL = 2000
FORCE_DECEL = 2000
FORCE_JERK = 50000
MOVEADD(40)
BASE(10)
MOVELINK(1000, 1000, 0, 0, 11)
WAIT UNTIL MARK OR IDLE
IF MARK THEN
adjustment_dist = calc_reg_adjust(REG_POS)
FORCE_SPEED = 150
FORCE_ACCEL = 2000
FORCE_DECEL = 2000
FORCE_JERK = 50000
MOVEADD(adjustment_dist)
missed_mark_count = 0
ELSE
missed_mark_count = missed_mark_count + 1
ENDIF

FORWARD
WA(100)
FORCE_SPEED = 50
FORCE_ACCEL = 2000
FORCE_DECEL = 2000
FORCE_JERK = 50000
MOVEADD(40)

BASE(10)
MOVELINK(1000, 1000, 0, 0, 11)
WAIT UNTIL MARK OR IDLE
IF MARK THEN
adjustment_dist = calc_reg_adjust(REG_POS)
FORCE_SPEED = 150
FORCE_ACCEL = 2000
FORCE_DECEL = 2000
FORCE_JERK = 50000
MOVEADD(adjustment_dist)
missed_mark_count = 0
ELSE
missed_mark_count = missed_mark_count + 1
ENDIF

name: MOVECIRC
description: This command moves two orthogonal axes in such a way as to produce a circular arc at the tool point. The length and radius of the arc are defined by the five parameters in the command line. The move parameters are always relative to the end of the last specified move. This is the start position on the circle circumference. Axis 1 is the current BASE axis. Axis 2 is the next axis in the BASE array. The first 4 distance parameters are scaled according to the current unit conversion factor for the BASE axis. An enhanced MOVECIRC function, using Transition Curves can be enabled by adding the transition angle and, optionally, the output offset parameters. This is available in firmware from version 2.0221 onwards. For the MOVECIRC command to be correctly executed, the two axes generating the circular arc must have the same number of encoder pulses/linear axis distance. If this is not the case it is possible to adjust the encoder scales in many cases by using ENCODER_RATIO or STEP_RATIO . If the end point specified is not on the circular arc. The arc will end at the angle specified by a line between the centre and the end point. Neither axis may cross the set absolute repeat distance ( REP_DIST ) during a MOVECIRC. Doing so may cause one or both axes to jump or for their FE value to exceed FE_LIMIT .
params:
Position on 
BASE
 axis to finish
  at.

Position on next axis in 
BASE

  array to finish at.

Position on 
BASE
 about which to
  move.

Position on next axis in 
BASE

  array about which to move.
Arc is interpolated in an anti-clockwise direction
Arc is interpolated in a clockwise direction
Arc is interpolated using the shortest path to endpoint
Arc is interpolated using the longest path to endpoint

Transition angle (in radians): the angle over which the
  circle should be transitioned into.

Option to output details of the transition curve to TABLE
  starting at TABLE(0)
Scale factor from transition curve calculations

Transition angle used (this can be less than requested
  if arc angle is insufficient)
examples:
MOVE(0, 6)                 'Move A -> B
MOVECIRC(3, 3, 3, 0, 1)    'Move B -> C
MOVE(2, 0)                 'Move C -> D
MOVECIRC(3, -3, 0, -3, 1)  'Move D -> E
MOVE(0, -6)                'Move E -> F
MOVECIRC(-3, -3, -3, 0, 1) 'Move F -> G
MOVE(-2, 0)                'Move G -> H
MOVECIRC(-3, 3, 0, 3, 1)   'Move H -> A
BASE(0, 1)
MOVEABS(0, 5)               'Move to position above first
tube
MOVEABS(0, 0)               'Lower for first drop
WAIT IDLE
OP(15, ON)                  'Apply dropper
WA(20)
OP(15, OFF)
FOR x = 0 TO 5
MOVECIRC(5, 0, 2.5, 0, 1) 'Arc between the test tubes
WAIT IDLE
OP(15, ON)                'Apply dropper
WA(20)
OP(15, OFF)
NEXT x
MOVECIRC(5, 5, 5, 0, 1)     'Move to rest position
ta = 10 * PI / 180 'Set the transition angle to 10 degrees
MOVEABS(0, 40)                    'Move A -> B
MOVECIRC(30, 30, 30, 0, 1, ta)    'Move B -> C
MOVE(20, 0)                       'Move C -> D
MOVECIRC(30, -30, 0, -30, 1, ta)  'Move D -> E
MOVE(0, -40)                      'Move E -> F
MOVECIRC(-30, -30, -30, 0, 1, ta) 'Move F -> G
MOVE(-20, 0)                      'Move G -> H
MOVECIRC(-30, 30, 0, 30, 1, ta)   'Move H -> A

MOVE(0, 6)                 'Move A -> B
MOVECIRC(3, 3, 3, 0, 1)    'Move B -> C
MOVE(2, 0)                 'Move C -> D
MOVECIRC(3, -3, 0, -3, 1)  'Move D -> E
MOVE(0, -6)                'Move E -> F
MOVECIRC(-3, -3, -3, 0, 1) 'Move F -> G
MOVE(-2, 0)                'Move G -> H
MOVECIRC(-3, 3, 0, 3, 1)   'Move H -> A

BASE(0, 1)
MOVEABS(0, 5)               'Move to position above first
tube
MOVEABS(0, 0)               'Lower for first drop
WAIT IDLE
OP(15, ON)                  'Apply dropper
WA(20)
OP(15, OFF)
FOR x = 0 TO 5
MOVECIRC(5, 0, 2.5, 0, 1) 'Arc between the test tubes
WAIT IDLE
OP(15, ON)                'Apply dropper
WA(20)
OP(15, OFF)
NEXT x
MOVECIRC(5, 5, 5, 0, 1)     'Move to rest position

ta = 10 * PI / 180 'Set the transition angle to 10 degrees
MOVEABS(0, 40)                    'Move A -> B
MOVECIRC(30, 30, 30, 0, 1, ta)    'Move B -> C
MOVE(20, 0)                       'Move C -> D
MOVECIRC(30, -30, 0, -30, 1, ta)  'Move D -> E
MOVE(0, -40)                      'Move E -> F
MOVECIRC(-30, -30, -30, 0, 1, ta) 'Move F -> G
MOVE(-20, 0)                      'Move G -> H
MOVECIRC(-30, 30, 0, 30, 1, ta)   'Move H -> A

name: MOVECIRCSP
description: Works as MOVECIRC and additionally allows vector speed to be changed when using multiple moves in the look ahead buffer when MERGE = ON , using additional parameters FORCE_SPEED and ENDMOVE_SPEED . Optional parameters ta and output allow the use of transition curves. See MOVECIRC .
examples:
FORCE_SPEED = 40
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 20
MOVECIRCSP(100, 100, 0, 100, 1)

name: MOVECONTOUR
syntax:
[value = ]MOVECONTOUR(table_pointer, axes, npoints, options,
speed_option, output_table_area, short_len, corner_acc)
description: The MOVECONTOUR command allows a sequence of 5 axis movements to be loaded via a set of TABLE values. A single move is loaded which interpolates the speed and the positions of the 5 axes simultaneously to allow for “contouring control”. Since the export of software for greater than 4 axes of contouring control is controlled by international treaties, this 5 axis MOVECONTOUR functionality is not built into Trio motion controllers. However, where an export licence is available, the software (product code P980) may be used to purchase 5 axis contouring control firmware. The 4-axis version can be used without the licence. MOVE_COUNT is incremented on every move loaded. This parameter may be set by a user program. The return value indicates various types of error and the number of vectors processed (see table below).
params: Location of the absolute points in TABLE memory.

The number of points, each point requires 2-6 table
  values. 6 are only used when a speed profile along a 5-axis path is also
  embedded in the TABLE.

0 = Process the move and load it


  

1 = Process the move but without loading it

Embed a speed profile together with axis position
  points in input TABLE data

Take initial direction of XYZ from END_DIR_LAST axis
  parameters (requires AXIS_MODE.2 = ON)

Specifies an offset between data points other than
  “Axes”. Note that this is ESSENTIAL if Bit 5 is set

Apply ACCEL and DECEL limits to the embedded speed
  profile

Calculate point radii and required speed at point (set
  automatically if corner_acc > 0)

Slow down, don’t return from MOVECONTOUR when
  STOP_ANGLE exceeded
Set initial values for (Bit 5) speed profile from SPEED

1 = B-spline interpolation


  

2 = C-spline interpolation


  

3 = Cubic spline

GCODE N field is copied into output table in lieu of W
  axis position
Return on angle change exceeding RAISE_ANGLE

Expand number of output points when length >
  short_len

Set to 1 to embed an initial speed for the start of the
  path in the data table.

Output of data to TABLE at this area. Controller will
  write 15 x Npoints of output data.

Optional parameter - Moves greater than this length in
  the TABLE entries will cause the MOVECONTOUR to finish. They can then
  normally be processed with MOVEABS.

Optional parameter – If specified, the speed entries in
  the TABLE can be reduced (not increased) depending on the effective radius
  between segments.

name: MOVELINK
description: The linked move command is designed for controlling movements such as: Synchronisation to conveyors Flying shears Thread chasing, tapping etc. Coil winding The motion consists of a linear movement with separately variable acceleration and deceleration phases linked via a software gearbox to the MEASURED position ( MPOS ) of another axis. The command uses the BASE and AXIS , and unit conversion factors in a similar way to other move commands. The “link” axis may move in either direction to drive the output motion. The link distances specified are always positive. Motion profiles Standard Trapezoidal profile Sinusoidal S-Curve (Bit 4 = 1 and Bits 10 & 11 = 0) Power 5 polynomial S-Curve (Bit 4 = 1 and Bits 10 & 11 = 3) Parameters: distance: incremental distance in user units to be moved on the current base axis, as a result of the measured movement on the “input” axis which drives the move. link dist: positive incremental distance in user units which is required to be measured on the “link” axis to result in the motion on the base axis. link acc: positive incremental distance in user units on the input axis over which the base axis accelerates. link dec: positive incremental distance in user units on the input axis over which the base axis decelerates. link axis: Specifies the axis to “link” to. It should be set to a value between 0 and the number of available axes. link_options: Bit value options to customise how MOVELINK operates Bit 0 Link commences exactly when registration event MARK occurs on link axis Bit 1 Link commences at an absolute position on link axis (see link_pos for start position) Bit 2 MOVELINK repeats automatically and bi-directionally when this bit is set. (This mode can be cleared by setting bit 1 of the REP_OPTION axis parameter) Bit 3 Reserved (see CAMBOX) Bit 4 If this bit is set the MOVELINK acceleration and deceleration phases are constructed using an curved speed profile rather than a trapezoidal speed profile. The curve type to use is specified in bits 10…12. Bit 5 Link is only active during a positive move on the link axis Bit 6 Reserved Bit 7 Reserved (see CAMBOX) Bit 8 Link commences exactly when registration event MARKB occurs on link axis Bit 9 Link commences exactly when registration event R_MARK occurs on link axis. (see link_pos for channel number) Bits 10…12 S-Ramp mode selection for use when bit 4 is set. Figure shows peak acceleration compared to linear ramp. Contains a 3-bit integer value that represents the type of curve for the excitation phase: 0 - SINE speed Profile (1.55) 1 - Power 9 polynomial speed profile (2.42) 2 - Power 7 polynomial speed profile (2.16) 3 - Power 5 polynomial speed profile (1.86) 4 – Linear ramp profile 5..7 – Unused Bit 13 Slave axis follows master axis DPOS (otherwise MPOS) Bit 14 Link is active during positive movement from a threshold. The threshold is updated to the highest positive value. link_pos: link_option bit 1 - the absolute position on the link axis in user UNITS where the MOVELINK is to be start. link_option bit 9 – the registration channel to start the movement on base_dist Firmware V2.0253 onwards. The “base distance” is the distance that is to be travelled at the base speed and is part of the total first “distance” parameter. See Examples 4 and 5 below. MOVELINK_MODIFY cannot be used when the base distance is in use. If the sum of parameter 3 and parameter 4 is greater than parameter 2, they are both reduced in proportion until they equal parameter 2. The link_dist is in the user units of the link axis and should always be specified as a positive distance. The link options for start (bits 1, 2, 8 and 9) may be combined with the link options for repeat (bits 4 and 8) and direction. start_pos cannot be at or within one servo period’s worth of movement of the REP_DIST position.
examples:
WHILE IN(2) = ON
MOVELINK(0, 150, 0, 0, 7)        'Dwell (no movement)
for 150m
MOVELINK(0.3, 0.6, 0.6, 0, 7)    'Accelerate to paper
speed
MOVELINK(0.7, 1.0, 0, 0.6, 7)    'Track the paper then
decelerate
WAIT LOADED 'Wait until acceleration movelink is
finished
OP(8, ON)   'Activate cutter
MOVELINK(-1.0, 8.4, 0.5, 0.5, 7) 'Retract cutter back to
start
WAIT LOADED
OP(8, OFF)  'Deactivate cutter at end of outward stroke
WEND
MOVELINK(0.3, 0.6, 0.6, 0, 1) 'Move is all accel
MOVELINK(0.4, 0.4, 0, 0, 1) 'All constant speed
MOVELINK(0.3, 0.6, 0, 0.6, 1) 'All decel
MOVELINK(1, 1.6, 0.6, 0.6, 1) 'Same as 3 moves above
MOVELINK(0.3, 0.6, 0.6, 0, 1)
MOVELINK(0.7, 1.0, 0, 0.6, 1)

WHILE IN(2) = ON
MOVELINK(0, 150, 0, 0, 7)        'Dwell (no movement)
for 150m
MOVELINK(0.3, 0.6, 0.6, 0, 7)    'Accelerate to paper
speed
MOVELINK(0.7, 1.0, 0, 0.6, 7)    'Track the paper then
decelerate
WAIT LOADED 'Wait until acceleration movelink is
finished
OP(8, ON)   'Activate cutter
MOVELINK(-1.0, 8.4, 0.5, 0.5, 7) 'Retract cutter back to
start
WAIT LOADED
OP(8, OFF)  'Deactivate cutter at end of outward stroke
WEND
MOVELINK(0.3, 0.6, 0.6, 0, 1) 'Move is all accel
MOVELINK(0.4, 0.4, 0, 0, 1) 'All constant speed
MOVELINK(0.3, 0.6, 0, 0.6, 1) 'All decel
MOVELINK(1, 1.6, 0.6, 0.6, 1) 'Same as 3 moves above
MOVELINK(0.3, 0.6, 0.6, 0, 1)
MOVELINK(0.7, 1.0, 0, 0.6, 1)

name: MOVELINK_MODIFY
description: MOVELINK_MODIFY allows MOVELINK motion commands to be modified whilst they are running by applying the specified positional offset. A typical example of using MOVELINK_MODIFY is for stopping product relative to a registration mark when using MOVELINK commands which link motion to a conveyor. The MOVELINK_MODIFY adjustment will only be applied if the MOVELINK has NOT already started decelerating and if there is sufficient time to make the adjustment without specified deceleration having to be faster than specified. If MOVELINK_MODIFY cannot be applied for the reasons specified, the parameter will remain at the requested adjustment value.
params:
Relative positional offset for the current MOVELINK to
  complete at.
examples:
MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0 'Ensure any earlier adjustments are
cleared
DEFPOS(0, 0) 'Makes the adjustment easier to understand
MOVELINK(18000, 10000, 1000, 1000, 1, 16)
TRIGGER
WAIT LOADED
PRINT ENDMOVE 'Prints 18000
WAIT UNTIL MPOS AXIS(1) > 2000 'Wait for some condition
MOVELINK_MODIFY = -7000 'Reduce the target by 7000
WAIT UNTIL MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0 'Wait until offset applied
PRINT ENDMOVE 'Prints 11000
MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0 'Ensure any earlier adjustments are
cleared


DEFPOS(0, 0) 'Makes the adjustment easier to understand


MOVELINK(0, 5000, 0, 0, 1) 'Delay for 5000 on axis 1


WAIT UNTIL MPOS AXIS(1) > 1000





MOVELINK_MODIFY = -1500


WAIT UNTIL MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0


WAIT IDLE


PRINT MPOS AXIS(1) 'Prints 3500 approximately
MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0


REGIST(3)


MOVELINK(19000, 20000, 1000, 1000, 1, 16)


WAIT LOADED


WAIT UNTIL MARK OR IDLE


IF MARK THEN


    MOVELINK_MODIFY = REG_POS + 5000 – ENDMOVE


ENDIF

MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0 'Ensure any earlier adjustments are
cleared
DEFPOS(0, 0) 'Makes the adjustment easier to understand
MOVELINK(18000, 10000, 1000, 1000, 1, 16)
TRIGGER
WAIT LOADED
PRINT ENDMOVE 'Prints 18000
WAIT UNTIL MPOS AXIS(1) > 2000 'Wait for some condition
MOVELINK_MODIFY = -7000 'Reduce the target by 7000
WAIT UNTIL MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0 'Wait until offset applied
PRINT ENDMOVE 'Prints 11000

MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0 'Ensure any earlier adjustments are
cleared


DEFPOS(0, 0) 'Makes the adjustment easier to understand


MOVELINK(0, 5000, 0, 0, 1) 'Delay for 5000 on axis 1


WAIT UNTIL MPOS AXIS(1) > 1000





MOVELINK_MODIFY = -1500


WAIT UNTIL MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0


WAIT IDLE


PRINT MPOS AXIS(1) 'Prints 3500 approximately

MOVELINK_MODIFY = 0


REGIST(3)


MOVELINK(19000, 20000, 1000, 1000, 1, 16)


WAIT LOADED


WAIT UNTIL MARK OR IDLE


IF MARK THEN


    MOVELINK_MODIFY = REG_POS + 5000 – ENDMOVE


ENDIF

name: MOVEMODIFY
description: MOVEMODIFY will change the absolute end position of a linear move immediately ahead of it, either the currently active move if the move buffer is empty, or the last move loaded into the move buffer. If there is neither a motion command active nor one in the move buffer, or the last move loaded is not a linear move, then a MOVEMODIFY move is loaded. A linear move type is one of MOVE , MOVEABS , MOVESP or MOVEABSSP . If there are multiple linear moves in the move buffer, then MOVEMODIFY will only modify the last move loaded. If the change in end position requires a change in direction, then CANCEL is issued.
params: Absolute position for the move to complete at.
examples:
MOVE(10000)           'Start a long move on conveyor
REGIST(3)             'Set up registration
WAIT UNTIL MARK       'MARK goes TRUE when sensor detects
glass edge
OFFPOS = -REG_POS     'Set position where mark was seen to
0
WAIT UNTIL OFFPOS = 0 'Wait for OFFPOS to take effect
MOVEMODIFY(250)       'Change move to stop at 250mm

MOVE(10000)           'Start a long move on conveyor
REGIST(3)             'Set up registration
WAIT UNTIL MARK       'MARK goes TRUE when sensor detects
glass edge
OFFPOS = -REG_POS     'Set position where mark was seen to
0
WAIT UNTIL OFFPOS = 0 'Wait for OFFPOS to take effect
MOVEMODIFY(250)       'Change move to stop at 250mm

name: MOVEPICK
syntax:
MOVEPICK(mode, x, y, z, withdraw, overlap_control_a, overlap_control_b,
u, v, w, withdraw_control, xy_control, approach_control)
description: MOVEPICK is a move type aimed at making it simple to program fast pick and place operations. Associated move type PICKLINK is a linked move equivalent to MOVEPICK that is linked to the measured motion on another axis in a similar way to MOVELINK or FLEXLINK. MOVEPICK is an absolute move to a position in 3..6 axes. However, the motion includes an incremental “withdraw” amount and special controls for the move speed profile to allow fast pick and place moves. Multi-axis interpolation uses the UNITS, SPEED, ACCEL and DECEL of the BASE axis.
params:
Specify the number of axes 0 = xyz, 1 = xyzu, 2 = xyzuv,
  3 = xyzuvw

Incremental withdraw distance from the start position
  on z axis
0..1 Adjust the speed for withdraw section (optional)
0..1 Adjust the speed for xy section (optional)
0..1 Adjust the speed for approach section (optional)

name: MOVESEQ
syntax:
MOVESEQ(table pointer, axes, npoints, options, radius[, output[,
transition_angle]])
description: The MOVESEQ command allows a sequence of movements to be loaded via TABLE values. The moves can be automatically merged together using a circular or spherical arc. Movement on 2 to 6 axes may be controlled in this way. The MOVESEQ is loaded into the controller move buffers as a sequence of MOVE->MOVECIRC-> moves if 2 axes are specified and MOVE->MSPHERICAL-> if 3 or more axes are specified. The linear move may be omitted if the arcs blend together. If “Options” bit 0 is set to 1 the move sequence loaded will be a sequence of MOVESP->MOVECIRCSP-> moves if 2 axes are specified and MOVESP->MSPHERICALSP-> if 3+ axes are specified. If Options bit 0 is set, 4 additional speed control values must be provided with each table point. MOVE_COUNT is incremented on every move loaded. This parameter may be set by a user program.
params:
Index in TABLE memory where the relative positions
  start.

The number of points, each point requires 2 or 3 table
  values.

0 = Sequence will load MOVEABS, MOVECIRC, etc.


  

1 = Sequence will load embedded speed moves MOVEABSSP,
  MOVECIRCSP, etc.

0 = No MOVE_PA/MOVE_PB data in TABLE


  

1 = MOVE_PA/MOVE_PB data is embedded within the TABLE
  data

0 = No embedded MOVETURN


  

1 = Embed MOVETURN (default is mode 5 unless bit 16 is
  set)

0 = Axes above XYZ are linear


  

1 = Axes above XYZ are in degrees

Contains a 7-bit integer that specifies an offset
  between data points other than "Axes".

0 = Use radius parameter


  

1 = Radius is embedded within the TABLE data

0 = Use MOVETURN mode 5 (default)


  

1 = Use MOVETURN mode 6

The radius of the corner fillet (see option bit 14,
  which can override the value supplied in this parameter)

Optional output of data to TABLE.  0 means do not
  output data to TABLE.  Value otherwise is the TABLE start index for the data.

Optional specification of transition angle (in Radians)
  for any filleted arcs that are generated.  Either do not supply a parameter
  or set it to 0 if no transition curve is to be applied.
examples:
FOR x = 0 TO 2


    BASE(x)


    ATYPE = 0


    UNITS = 100


    ACCEL = 500


    DECEL = ACCEL


    SERVO = ON


    SPEED = 100


NEXT x





BASE(0, 1, 2)





DEFPOS(100, 0, 0)





TABLE(1000, -100, 0, 0)


TABLE(1003, 0, 200, 0)


TABLE(1006, 200, 0, 0)


TABLE(1009, 0, 200, 0)


TABLE(1012, 150, 0, 0)


TABLE(1015, -50, -400, 0)


TABLE(1018, -300, -200, 0)





TRIGGER


WA(10)





MOVESEQ(1000, 3, 7, 0, 300)





WAIT IDLE

FOR x = 0 TO 2


    BASE(x)


    ATYPE = 0


    UNITS = 100


    ACCEL = 500


    DECEL = ACCEL


    SERVO = ON


    SPEED = 100


NEXT x





BASE(0, 1, 2)





DEFPOS(100, 0, 0)





TABLE(1000, -100, 0, 0)


TABLE(1003, 0, 200, 0)


TABLE(1006, 200, 0, 0)

TABLE(1018, -300, -200, 0)





TRIGGER


WA(10)





MOVESEQ(1000, 3, 7, 0, 300)





WAIT IDLE

name: MOVESP
description: Works as MOVE and additionally allows vector speed to be changed when using multiple moves in the look ahead buffer when MERGE = ON , using additional parameters FORCE_SPEED , ENDMOVE_SPEED and STARTMOVE_SPEED . Multi-axis interpolation uses the UNITS, SPEED, ACCEL and DECEL of the BASE axis. For correct XYZ vector operation, the UNITS of all 3 axes must be the same.
params: distance to move on base axis from current position.

distance to move on next axis in 
BASE

  array from current position.

distance to move on next axis in 
BASE

  array from current position.

distance to move on next axis in 
BASE

  array from current position.
examples:
FORCE_SPEED = 40
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 20
MOVESP(100, 100)

name: MOVESPCONT
description: Under development, use only on instruction from Trio.
params: Distance to move on base axis from current position.

name: MOVES_BUFFERED
description: This returns the number of moves being buffered by the axis. The value does not include the move in the MTYPE buffer.
params: number of commands in the move buffers.
examples:
IF MOVES_BUFFERED < 64 THEN
xpos = TABLE(count + x)
ypos = TABLE(count + y)
MOVEABS(xpos, ypos)
count = count + 1
ENDIF

name: MOVETANG
description: Moves the axis to the required position using the programmed SPEED , ACCEL and DECEL for the axis. The direction of movement is determined by a calculation of the shortest path to the position assuming that the axis is rotating and that REP_DIST has been set to PI radians (180 degrees) and that REP_OPTION = 0. The REP_DIST value will depend on the UNITS value and the number of steps representing PI radians. For example if the rotary axis has 4000 pulses/turn and UNITS = 1 the REP_DIST value would be 2000. MOVETANG does not get cleared from the MTYPE when it has completed its movement. This is so that you can use it in a tight loop which updates the end position by calling the MOVETANG again. When using the link_axis the end position is automatically updated from TANG_DIRECTION of the link axis.
params:
The absolute position to be set as the endpoint of the
  move. Value must be within the range –
PI
 to +
PI
 in the units of the rotary axis. For example, if the
  rotary axis has 4000 pulses/turn, the 
UNITS
 value=1
  and the angle required is 
PI
/2 (90 degrees) the
  position value would be 1000.

An optional link axis may be specified. When a
  link_axis is specified the system software calculates the absolute position
  required each servo cycle based on the link axis 
TANG_DIRECTION
.
  The 
TANG_DIRECTION
 is multiplied by the 
REP_DIST
/
PI
 to calculate the
  required  position. Note that when using a link_axis the absolute_position
  parameter becomes unused. The position is copied every servo cycle until the
  MOVETANG is 
CANCEL
led.
examples:
BASE(0, 1)
WHILE TRUE
angle = TANG_DIRECTION
MOVETANG(angle) AXIS(2)
WEND
MOVETANG(0, 4) AXIS(2)
'Set up 3 axes for Tangential Control
WDOG = OFF
BASE(0)
P_GAIN = 0.9
VFF_GAIN = 12.85
UNITS = 50  'Set units for mm
SERVO = ON
BASE(1)
P_GAIN = 0.9
VFF_GAIN = 12.30
UNITS = 50 'Units must be the same for both axes
SERVO = ON
BASE(2)
UNITS = 1  'Make units 1 for the setting of rep_dist
REP_DIST = 2000 'Encoder has 4000 edges per rev.
REP_OPTION = 0
UNITS = 4000 / (2 * PI) 'Set units for Radians
SERVO = ON
WDOG = ON
'Home the 3rd axis to its Z mark
DATUM(1) AXIS(2)
WAIT IDLE
WA(10)
'Start the tangential control routine
BASE(0, 1) 'Define the pair of axes which are for X and Y
'Start the tangential control
BASE(2)
MOVETANG(0, 0) 'Use axes 0 and 1 as the linked pair
'Program to cut a square shape with rounded corners
MERGE = ON
SPEED = 300
nobuf = FALSE 'When true, the moves are not buffered
size = 120    'Size of each side of the square
c = 30        'Radius of quarter circles at each corner
DEFPOS(0, 0)
WAIT UNTIL OFFPOS = 0
WA(10)
MOVEABS(10, 10 + c)
REPEAT
MOVE(0, size)
MOVECIRC(c, c, c, 0, 1)
IF nobuf THEN WAIT IDLE: WA(2)
MOVE(size, 0)
MOVECIRC(c, -c, 0, -c, 1)
IF nobuf THEN WAIT IDLE: WA(2)
MOVE(0, -size)
MOVECIRC(-c, -c, -c, 0, 1)
IF nobuf THEN WAIT IDLE: WA(2)
MOVE(-size, 0)
MOVECIRC(-c, c, 0, c, 1)
IF nobuf THEN WAIT IDLE: WA(2)
UNTIL FALSE

BASE(0, 1)
WHILE TRUE
angle = TANG_DIRECTION
MOVETANG(angle) AXIS(2)
WEND
MOVETANG(0, 4) AXIS(2)

'Set up 3 axes for Tangential Control
WDOG = OFF
BASE(0)
P_GAIN = 0.9
VFF_GAIN = 12.85
UNITS = 50  'Set units for mm
SERVO = ON
BASE(1)
P_GAIN = 0.9
VFF_GAIN = 12.30
UNITS = 50 'Units must be the same for both axes
SERVO = ON
BASE(2)

REP_DIST = 2000 'Encoder has 4000 edges per rev.
REP_OPTION = 0
UNITS = 4000 / (2 * PI) 'Set units for Radians
SERVO = ON
WDOG = ON
'Home the 3rd axis to its Z mark
DATUM(1) AXIS(2)
WAIT IDLE
WA(10)
'Start the tangential control routine
BASE(0, 1) 'Define the pair of axes which are for X and Y
'Start the tangential control
BASE(2)
MOVETANG(0, 0) 'Use axes 0 and 1 as the linked pair
'Program to cut a square shape with rounded corners
MERGE = ON
SPEED = 300
nobuf = FALSE 'When true, the moves are not buffered
size = 120    'Size of each side of the square
c = 30        'Radius of quarter circles at each corner
DEFPOS(0, 0)
WAIT UNTIL OFFPOS = 0
WA(10)
MOVEABS(10, 10 + c)
REPEAT
MOVE(0, size)
MOVECIRC(c, c, c, 0, 1)
IF nobuf THEN WAIT IDLE: WA(2)
MOVE(size, 0)
MOVECIRC(c, -c, 0, -c, 1)
IF nobuf THEN WAIT IDLE: WA(2)
MOVE(0, -size)
MOVECIRC(-c, -c, -c, 0, 1)
IF nobuf THEN WAIT IDLE: WA(2)
MOVE(-size, 0)
MOVECIRC(-c, c, 0, c, 1)
IF nobuf THEN WAIT IDLE: WA(2)
UNTIL FALSE

name: MOVETEST
syntax:
MOVETEST(start/stop, dist0, accel0, speed0, dwell0, dist1,
accel1, speed1, dwell1)
description: Start/stop a test move. This will perform a continuous cycle of 2 moves based on the profile parameters in the function. The axis will move forward and backward cyclically over the two selected distances. Each movement has its own acceleration and speed values which are in user units. The dwell between moves is in milliseconds. All motion stopping command like CANCEL, RAPIDSTOP will stop the motion Calling MOVETEST(1, ...) will start the motion Calling MOVETEST(0, ...) will stop the motion at the end of the second move
params: Stop the motion at the end of the second move
Distance to move in the forward direction.
Acceleration applied to the forward move.
Speed applied to the forward move.

Dwell time in milliseconds after the forward movement
  before starting the reverse move.
Distance to move in the reverse direction.
Acceleration applied to the reverse move.
Speed applied to the reverse move.

Dwell time in milliseconds after the reverse movement
  before starting the next forward move.

name: MOVETURN
syntax:
MOVETURN(mode, axes, x_start, y_start, x_end, y_end, sp_start, sp_end[,
acc[, x_po[, y_po[, sp_lim]]]])


MOVETURN(mode, axes, x_start, y_start, z_start, x_end, y_end, z_end[,
sp_start[, x_po[, y_po[, sp_lim]]]])
params: Equivalent of mode 1, but for 3 axes

name: MOVE_COUNT
description: MOVE_COUNT increments every time a motion command loads into the MTYPE buffer or when a command is automatically re-loaded such as MOVELINK , CAMBOX and FLEXLINK when using option value 4. (Auto Repeat) MOVE_COUNT can be written to set an initial value.
examples:
MOVE_COUNT = 0
RUN "MOTION"
WAIT UNTIL MOVE_COUNT > 10
OP(11,ON)
motion_axis = 0
link_axis = 1
BASE(motion_axis)
MOVE_COUNT = 0
MOVELINK(10,12,2,2,link_axis,4)
IF IDLE AXIS(link_axis) THEN
FORWARD AXIS(link_axis)
ENDIF
WAIT UNTIL MOVE_COUNT >= 250
REP_OPTION.1 = 1
WAIT IDLE

MOVE_COUNT = 0
RUN "MOTION"
WAIT UNTIL MOVE_COUNT > 10
OP(11,ON)

motion_axis = 0
link_axis = 1
BASE(motion_axis)
MOVE_COUNT = 0
MOVELINK(10,12,2,2,link_axis,4)
IF IDLE AXIS(link_axis) THEN
FORWARD AXIS(link_axis)
ENDIF
WAIT UNTIL MOVE_COUNT >= 250
REP_OPTION.1 = 1
WAIT IDLE

name: MOVE_COUNT_INC
description: MOVE_COUNT_INC defines the value that MOVE_COUNT will change every time a motion command is loaded and starts. The setting can be used when a pattern of moves is stopped and then reversed, for example in a machine following a series of points through an XY path.

name: MOVE_FORCE_ACCEL
description: Returns the current move’s FORCE_ACCEL value.

name: MOVE_FORCE_DECEL
description: Returns the current move’s FORCE_DECEL value.

name: MOVE_FORCE_SPEED
description: Returns the current move’s FORCE_SPEED value.

name: MOVE_PA
description: MOVE_PA is a special axis parameter which pass through the motion buffers and become available during the motion for processing. This and MOVE_PB are two axis parameters which can be freely set by BASIC. Every time a move is loaded by BASIC a copy is made of both these parameters into the move queue buffers. This parameter is used to switch outputs linked to moves. For example switching ON a certain output once a move command is executed. Every time a move is loaded by BASIC a copy is made of both these parameters into the move queue buffers:
params: Set the output number to be switched

The value in MOVE_PA is placed in AXIS_DEBUG_A
  synchronised with the loading of the move.
The number of outputs to be set/cleared
examples:
SPEED = 15
FORCE_SPEED = 15
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 15


MERGE = ON


LIMIT_BUFFERED = 8


ACCEL = SPEED * 20
DECEL = SPEED * 20


TRIGGER
MOVE_PA = 10
MOVE_PA_CONT = 1
MOVE_PA.12 = ON


MOVESP(200)


MOVE_PA.12 = OFF


MOVESP(20) 


MOVE_PA.12 = ON


MOVESP(100)
SPEED = 15
FORCE_SPEED = 15
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 15
MOVE_PA_CONT = 4
MOVE_PA = ($AA << 20) + (8 << 12) + 8


MOVE(200)

SPEED = 15
FORCE_SPEED = 15
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 15


MERGE = ON


LIMIT_BUFFERED = 8


ACCEL = SPEED * 20
DECEL = SPEED * 20


TRIGGER
MOVE_PA = 10
MOVE_PA_CONT = 1
MOVE_PA.12 = ON


MOVESP(200)


MOVE_PA.12 = OFF


MOVESP(20) 


MOVE_PA.12 = ON


MOVESP(100)

SPEED = 15
FORCE_SPEED = 15
ENDMOVE_SPEED = 15
MOVE_PA_CONT = 4
MOVE_PA = ($AA << 20) + (8 << 12) + 8


MOVE(200)

name: MOVE_PA_CONT
description: This parameter and MOVE_PB_CONT are two axis parameters which control what the system software does with MOVE_PA / MOVE_PB when the move becomes active or MOVE_PA_IDLE / MOVE_PB_IDLE when the axis becomes idle. By changing the value of this parameter, the behaviour of the MOVE_PA/MOVE_PB and MOVE_PA_IDLE/MOVE_PB_IDLE will change.
params:
Bits 0..11 of MOVE_PA set output #; Bit 12 sets ON/OFF.
  Output set to setting in MOVE_PA_IDLE when MTYPE = 0

Bits 0..11 of MOVE_PA set output #; Bit 12 sets
  ON/OFF.  Output set to setting in MOVE_PA_IDLE when VP_SPEED = 0 or
  MTYPE = 0

The bit pattern in MOVE_PA is copied to the outputs
  starting at a given index.  The bit pattern defined in MOVE_PA_IDLE is output
  when MTYPE = 0.

Analogue channel in MOVE_PA bits 32..39 set to 32-bit value
  in MOVE_PA bits 0..31 when move is started.

Analogue channel in MOVE_PA bits 32..39 set to 32-bit
  value in MOVE_PA bits 0..31 when move is started. MOVE_PA_IDLE is used when
  move is complete.

NEXT a

MERGE = ON


LIMIT_BUFFERED = 16


ACC(SPEED * 20)

MOVE_PA_CONT = 1
MOVE_PA.12 = ON


MOVE(200)


MOVE_PA.12 = OFF


MOVE(20) 


MOVE_PA.12 = ON

MOVE_PA_IDLE = 10


MOVE_PA_IDLE.12 = OFF
MOVE(20)


WAIT IDLE


WA(2000)


MOVE(20)


WA(2000)


SPEED = 0


MOVE(20)


WAIT IDLE


WA(2000)


MOVE(10)

FOR a = 0 TO 1


    UNITS AXIS(a )= 10


NEXT a

MERGE = ON


LIMIT_BUFFERED = 16
